records.taboo: 忌送带有明显政治色彩的传单
records.reason: 容易引起社交反感
records.alternative: 送中性的伴手礼

records.taboo: Avoid leaflets with obvious political overtones
records.reason: Easy to arouse social antipathy
records.alternative: Give neutral souvenirs

records.taboo: 忌送已凋谢或品质差的花束
records.reason: 被视为侮辱和没诚意
records.alternative: 确保鲜花新鲜、含苞待放

records.taboo: Avoid withered or poor-quality bouquets
records.reason: Regarded as an insult and lack of sincerity
records.alternative: Ensure the flowers are fresh and in bud

records.taboo: 忌送红玫瑰给普通朋友
records.reason: 红玫瑰仅代表强烈的浪漫爱情
records.alternative: 选择百合、向日葵或郁金香（避开白菊花）

records.taboo: Avoid giving red roses to ordinary friends
records.reason: Red roses only represent strong romantic love
records.alternative: Choose lilies, sunflowers or tulips (avoid white chrysanthemums)

records.taboo: 忌送尖锐物品（如刀具、剪刀）
records.reason: 迷信认为这会切断双方的友谊
records.alternative: 若送此类物品，收礼人需回赠一枚硬币表示"买下"

records.taboo: Avoid sharp items (such as knives, scissors)
records.reason: Superstition holds that this will cut off the friendship between the two parties
records.alternative: If giving such items, the recipient needs to give a coin in return to mean "buying it"

records.taboo: 忌送康乃馨给别人（尤其是生病的人）
records.reason: 在德国部分地区，康乃馨常用于葬礼
records.alternative: 选择色彩明丽的杂色花束

records.taboo: Avoid giving carnations to others (especially the sick)
records.reason: Carnations are often used for funerals in some parts of Germany
records.alternative: Choose bright and mixed-color bouquets

records.taboo: 忌送带有公司巨大Logo的廉价礼品
records.reason: 德国人看重品质而非品牌展示
records.alternative: 送高品质的手工艺品或实用好物

records.taboo: Avoid cheap gifts with the company's huge Logo
records.reason: Germans value quality over brand display
records.alternative: Give high-quality handicrafts or practical good things

records.taboo: 忌送菊花（Chrysanthemen）作为访友礼物
records.reason: 菊花在德国通常用于墓地和祭祀
records.alternative: 选择色彩鲜艳的时令花卉

records.taboo: Avoid giving chrysanthemums as a gift for visiting friends
records.reason: Chrysanthemums are usually used for cemeteries and sacrifices in Germany
records.alternative: Choose bright seasonal flowers

records.taboo: 忌送带有明显宗教色彩的礼物给不信教的人
records.reason: 德国社会高度世俗化
records.alternative: 送中性的、艺术类的礼物

records.taboo: Avoid giving gifts with obvious religious overtones to non-religious people
records.reason: German society is highly secularized
records.alternative: Give neutral and artistic gifts

records.taboo: 忌送带有人造香精味极浓的蜡烛
records.reason: 德国人偏向天然材料和极简生活
records.alternative: 选择天然大豆蜡或无香蜡烛

records.taboo: Avoid candles with extremely strong artificial fragrance
records.reason: Germans prefer natural materials and a minimalist life
records.alternative: Choose natural soy wax or unscented candles

records.taboo: 忌送带有明显政治党派色彩的传单或纪念品
records.reason: 容易引起社交反感
records.alternative: 送中性的、无政治暗示的伴手礼

records.taboo: Avoid leaflets or souvenirs with obvious political party overtones
records.reason: Easy to arouse social antipathy
records.alternative: Give neutral souvenirs without political implications

records.taboo: 忌送13这个数量的礼品或在13号送礼
records.reason: 13在德国文化中被视为不吉利的数字，与宗教传说中的背叛故事相关
records.alternative: 选择12或14等吉利数字的礼品，避开13号这一天送礼

records.taboo: Avoid giving 13 gifts or giving gifts on the 13th
records.reason: 13 is regarded as an unlucky number in German culture, related to the betrayal story in religious legends
records.alternative: Choose gifts with auspicious numbers such as 12 or 14, avoid giving gifts on the 13th

records.taboo: 忌送白菊花作为礼品
records.reason: 白菊花在德国是葬礼和悼念场合的专用花，用于日常送礼非常不吉利
records.alternative: 选择玫瑰（避开红玫瑰给非恋人）、郁金香、百合等吉祥花卉

records.taboo: Avoid giving white chrysanthemums as gifts
records.reason: White chrysanthemums are exclusive flowers for funerals and mourning occasions in Germany, and are very unlucky for daily gift-giving
records.alternative: Choose auspicious flowers such as roses (avoid red roses for non-lovers), tulips, lilies

records.taboo: 忌送过于私人的用品如内衣、化妆品给不熟悉的人
records.reason: 德国人注重个人隐私，过于私人的礼品会让人感到尴尬和不适
records.alternative: 送书籍、音乐CD、巧克力或高品质葡萄酒（需了解对方喜好）

records.taboo: Avoid giving overly personal items such as underwear and cosmetics to unfamiliar people
records.reason: Germans value personal privacy; overly personal gifts will make people feel embarrassed and uncomfortable
records.alternative: Give books, music CDs, chocolate or high-quality wine (need to understand the recipient's preferences)

records.taboo: 忌送包装粗糙或没有包装的礼品
records.reason: 德国人注重细节和品质，包装的好坏代表对对方的尊重程度
records.alternative: 使用高质量包装纸或礼品盒，搭配丝带等装饰提升精致度

records.taboo: Avoid giving roughly packaged or unpackaged gifts
records.reason: Germans pay attention to details and quality; the quality of packaging represents the degree of respect for the other party
records.alternative: Use high-quality wrapping paper or gift boxes, match with ribbons and other decorations to enhance exquisiteness

records.taboo: 忌在商务场合送过于贵重的礼品
records.reason: 贵重礼品可能被视为行贿行为，违反商务道德和相关规定
records.alternative: 送中等价值的商务礼品如钢笔、笔记本或公司定制纪念品

records.taboo: Avoid giving overly expensive gifts in business occasions
records.reason: Expensive gifts may be regarded as bribery, violating business ethics and relevant regulations
records.alternative: Give medium-value business gifts such as fountain pens, notebooks or company custom souvenirs

records.taboo: 忌送带有纳粹符号或相关的任何物品
records.reason: 纳粹符号在德国是非法的，代表历史悲剧，绝对禁止传播
records.alternative: 送德国传统文化相关礼品，如巴伐利亚风格的工艺品或啤酒杯

records.taboo: Avoid any items with Nazi symbols or related content
records.reason: Nazi symbols are illegal in Germany, representing historical tragedies and are absolutely prohibited from being spread
records.alternative: Give gifts related to German traditional culture, such as Bavarian-style handicrafts or beer mugs

records.taboo: 忌送钟表作为礼品
records.reason: 钟表象征时间的流逝，有"送终"的谐音联想，被视为不吉利
records.alternative: 送日历、planners 或其他实用的时间管理工具

records.taboo: Avoid giving clocks and watches as gifts
records.reason: Clocks and watches symbolize the passage of time, with the homophonic association of "sending to the end", regarded as unlucky
records.alternative: Give calendars, planners or other practical time management tools

records.taboo: 忌送未拆封的食品作为礼物时不附带发票
records.reason: 德国人注重食品安全和消费权益，保留发票便于退换有质量问题的食品
records.alternative: 赠送食品礼盒时，将发票单独封装在信封中一并送上

records.taboo: Avoid giving unopened food as gifts without an invoice
records.reason: Germans value food safety and consumer rights; keeping the invoice is convenient for returning or exchanging food with quality problems
records.alternative: When giving food gift boxes, seal the invoice separately in an envelope and send it together

records.taboo: 忌送印有纳粹时期地名或建筑的礼品
records.reason: 涉及纳粹历史敏感内容，易引起反感和争议，违背历史认知
records.alternative: 送现代德国著名建筑或风景的纪念品

records.taboo: Avoid gifts printed with place names or buildings of the Nazi period
records.reason: Involves sensitive content of Nazi history, easy to arouse resentment and controversy, violating historical cognition
records.alternative: Give souvenirs of famous modern German buildings or landscapes

records.taboo: 忌送印有错误地图（如边界错误）的礼品
records.reason: 地图边界错误涉及领土主权问题，易引起政治争议
records.alternative: 送官方认证的正确地图或地理科普礼品

records.taboo: Avoid gifts printed with wrong maps (such as border errors)
records.reason: Map border errors involve territorial sovereignty issues and are easy to arouse political disputes
records.alternative: Give officially certified correct maps or geography popular science gifts

records.taboo: 忌送在公共场合使用会引起注意的夸张礼品
records.reason: 德国人注重低调内敛，夸张礼品易让对方感到不自在
records.alternative: 送设计简约、实用性强的低调礼品

records.taboo: Avoid exaggerated gifts that attract attention when used in public
records.reason: Germans pay attention to low-key and restraint; exaggerated gifts easily make the other party feel uncomfortable
records.alternative: Give low-key gifts with simple design and strong practicality

records.taboo: 忌送味道过于辛辣或刺激的食品给不熟悉的人
records.reason: 德国人饮食偏清淡，辛辣刺激食品可能不符合口味，易引起肠胃不适
records.alternative: 送口味温和的传统德国食品或国际通用口味食品

records.taboo: Avoid giving overly spicy or stimulating food to unfamiliar people
records.reason: Germans have a light diet; spicy and stimulating food may not suit the taste and easily cause gastrointestinal discomfort
records.alternative: Give mild-tasting traditional German food or internationally universal taste food

records.taboo: 忌送包装上有拼写或语法错误的礼品
records.reason: 德国人注重严谨性，文字错误显得不专业、缺乏诚意
records.alternative: 送无文字或文字经过校对的礼品

records.taboo: Avoid gifts with spelling or grammatical errors on the packaging
records.reason: Germans pay attention to rigor; text errors seem unprofessional and lack sincerity
records.alternative: Give gifts without text or with proofread text

records.taboo: 忌送在宗教节日（如圣诞节）过于商业化的礼品
records.reason: 德国人注重宗教节日的传统意义，过度商业化显得缺乏诚意
records.alternative: 送具有传统节日意义的手工礼品

records.taboo: Avoid overly commercial gifts on religious festivals (such as Christmas)
records.reason: Germans value the traditional significance of religious festivals; excessive commercialization seems to lack sincerity
records.alternative: Give handcrafted gifts with traditional festival significance

records.taboo: 忌送带有儿童噪音过大的玩具
records.reason: 噪音过大影响家庭和邻里休息，不符合环保和邻里和谐理念
records.alternative: 送噪音小或可调节音量的益智玩具

records.taboo: Avoid children's toys with excessive noise
records.reason: Excessive noise affects the rest of families and neighbors, not in line with the concept of environmental protection and neighborhood harmony
records.alternative: Give puzzle toys with low noise or adjustable volume

records.taboo: 忌在商务会议开始前匆忙赠送廉价礼品
records.reason: 匆忙赠送显得准备不足，廉价礼品缺乏诚意，影响商务信任
records.alternative: 在会议合适时机赠送精心准备的中等价值礼品

records.taboo: Avoid hastily giving cheap gifts before the start of a business meeting
records.reason: Hasty gifting seems unprepared, and cheap gifts lack sincerity, affecting business trust
records.alternative: Give carefully prepared medium-value gifts at the right time of the meeting
records.source: Etiquette for the timing of business gifts

records.taboo: 忌送需要复杂安装且无德语说明书的商务礼品
records.reason: 无德语说明书导致安装困难，复杂安装增加时间成本，不符合实用主义
records.alternative: 送易安装且有德语说明书的商务礼品

records.taboo: Avoid business gifts that require complex installation without German instructions
records.reason: No German instructions lead to difficult installation, and complex installation increases time costs, not in line with pragmatism
records.alternative: Give easy-to-install business gifts with German instructions

records.taboo: 忌送需要定期维护且维护费用高的商务礼品
records.reason: 维护费用高增加对方成本，不符合商务礼品的实用原则
records.alternative: 送无需维护或维护成本低的商务礼品

records.taboo: Avoid business gifts that require regular maintenance and high maintenance costs
records.reason: High maintenance costs increase the other party's costs, not in line with the practical principle of business gifts
records.alternative: Give business gifts without maintenance or with low maintenance costs

records.taboo: 忌送需要提前预约且流程复杂的服务类商务礼品
records.reason: 流程复杂易让对方放弃使用，失去礼品价值，影响商务关系
records.alternative: 送预约简单或无需预约的服务类礼品

records.taboo: Avoid service-based business gifts that require advance reservation and complex procedures
records.reason: Complex procedures make it easy for the other party to give up using them, losing the gift value and affecting business relations
records.alternative: Give service gifts with simple reservation or no reservation required

records.taboo: 忌送需要频繁更换电池且电池非通用型号的小电器
records.reason: 非通用电池难购买，频繁更换增加使用成本，不符合实用主义
records.alternative: 送充电式或使用通用电池的小电器

records.taboo: Avoid small electrical appliances that require frequent battery replacement with non-universal battery models
records.reason: Non-universal batteries are hard to buy, and frequent replacement increases usage costs, not in line with pragmatism
records.alternative: Give rechargeable small electrical appliances or those using universal batteries

records.taboo: 忌送含有大量糖分且无营养成分表的零食
records.reason: 德国人注重健康，无营养成分表无法判断健康程度，高糖不利于健康
records.alternative: 送低糖且营养成分表清晰的健康零食

records.taboo: Avoid snacks with a lot of sugar and no nutrition label
records.reason: Germans value health; no nutrition label makes it impossible to judge the health level, and high sugar is not conducive to health
records.alternative: Give low-sugar healthy snacks with clear nutrition labels

records.taboo: 忌送尺寸标准不明确的服装礼品
records.reason: 尺寸不明确易导致不合身，退换麻烦，显得不够贴心
records.alternative: 送尺寸标准清晰或可调节尺寸的服装

records.taboo: Avoid clothing gifts with unclear size standards
records.reason: Unclear size standards are easy to cause ill-fitting, troublesome return and exchange, and seem not intimate enough
records.alternative: Give clothing with clear size standards or adjustable size

records.taboo: 忌送带有强烈气味的香皂或沐浴露
records.reason: 强烈气味可能不符合对方喜好，部分人对香味敏感易引起不适
records.alternative: 送无香或淡香的天然成分洗护用品

records.taboo: Avoid soap or shower gel with a strong smell
records.reason: Strong smell may not meet the other party's preferences, and some people are sensitive to fragrance and easy to feel uncomfortable
records.alternative: Give unscented or lightly scented washing and care products with natural ingredients

records.taboo: 忌送印有模糊或低分辨率图案的装饰用品
records.reason: 德国人注重品质，模糊图案显得廉价，不符合审美要求
records.alternative: 送高分辨率清晰图案的装饰用品

records.taboo: Avoid decorative items printed with blurry or low-resolution patterns
records.reason: Germans value quality; blurry patterns seem cheap and do not meet aesthetic requirements
records.alternative: Give decorative items with high-resolution clear patterns

records.taboo: 忌送保质期短且购买渠道少的进口食品
records.reason: 保质期短易过期，购买渠道少无法补货，造成浪费
records.alternative: 送保质期长且本地可购买的进口食品

records.taboo: Avoid imported food with a short shelf life and few purchase channels
records.reason: Short shelf life is easy to expire, and few purchase channels make it impossible to restock, causing waste
records.alternative: Give imported food with a long shelf life and available for local purchase

records.taboo: 忌送材质易损坏且难以修复的礼品
records.reason: 易损坏材质降低礼品使用寿命，难以修复导致浪费，不符合耐用理念
records.alternative: 送材质耐用且易修复的礼品

records.taboo: Avoid gifts made of easily damaged and hard-to-repair materials
records.reason: Easily damaged materials reduce the service life of gifts, and hard-to-repair leads to waste, not in line with the concept of durability
records.alternative: Give gifts made of durable and easy-to-repair materials

records.taboo: 忌送含有转基因成分且无明确标注的食品
records.reason: 德国人对转基因食品态度谨慎，无标注不符合食品透明化要求
records.alternative: 送非转基因且标注清晰的有机食品

records.taboo: Avoid food containing genetically modified ingredients without clear labeling
records.reason: Germans are cautious about genetically modified food, and no labeling does not meet the requirements of food transparency
records.alternative: Give non-genetically modified organic food with clear labeling

records.taboo: 忌送带有反光度过高且易造成光污染的装饰用品
records.reason: 高反光易造成光污染，影响视力和居住环境，不符合环保理念
records.alternative: 送反光度适中的环保装饰用品

records.taboo: Avoid decorative items with excessively high reflectivity that easily cause light pollution
records.reason: High reflectivity is easy to cause light pollution, affecting eyesight and living environment, not in line with the concept of environmental protection
records.alternative: Give environmentally friendly decorative items with moderate reflectivity

records.taboo: 不能送鞋给恋人
records.reason: 谐音“踏碎”缘分，寓意让对方“离开”
records.alternative: 送室内拖鞋/袜子

records.taboo: 恋人に靴を贈ってはならない
records.reason: 「縁を踏み砕く」と音が似ており、相手に「離れていってほしい」という意味合いになる
records.alternative: ルームシューズや靴下を贈る

records.taboo: 不能送梳子
records.reason: 发音“くし”与“苦死”同音，寓意苦难
records.alternative: 送发簪/发带

records.taboo: 櫛を贈ってはならない
records.reason: 「くし」の発音が「苦しむ」と同じで、災難を意味する
records.alternative: かんざしやヘアバンドを贈る

records.taboo: 不能送荷花
records.reason: 多用于葬礼，象征哀悼
records.alternative: 送樱花/菊花（非皇室用白菊）

records.taboo: ハスを贈ってはならない
records.reason: 葬式に多用され、哀悼を象徴する
records.alternative: サクラや菊（皇室専用の白菊を除く）を贈る

records.taboo: 不能送数量为4或9的礼物
records.reason: 4发音“し”=死，9发音“く”=苦
records.alternative: 送3/5/7等奇数数量

records.taboo: 数量が4または9の贈り物を贈ってはならない
records.reason: 4の発音「し」は「死」、9の発音「く」は「苦」に対応する
records.alternative: 3/5/7などの奇数の数量を贈る
records.source: ビジネスマナーハンドブック

records.taboo: 不能单独送手帕
records.reason: 传统上与葬礼擦泪相关，有离别之意
records.alternative: 搭配糖果/点心作为伴手礼

records.taboo: ハンカチを単独で贈ってはならない
records.reason: 伝統的に葬式の涙拭きに関連し、別離の意味がある
records.alternative: 菓子などと組み合わせてお土産として贈る

records.taboo: 不能用绿色/紫色包装礼物
records.reason: 绿色曾代表不祥，紫色关联悲伤
records.alternative: 用红/金/白包装（红白喜庆）

records.taboo: 緑や紫の包装で贈り物をすることは禁じられている
records.reason: 緑はかつて不吉を意味し、紫は悲しみに関連する
records.alternative: 赤/金/白で包装する（赤と白は祝いの色）

records.taboo: 不能送偶数金额礼金
records.reason: 偶数易被平分，象征分离
records.alternative: 送1万/3万/5万日元奇数金额

records.taboo: 偶数金額の祝儀を贈ってはならない
records.reason: 偶数は分けられやすく、分離を象徴する
records.alternative: 1万/3万/5万円などの奇数金額を贈る

records.taboo: 不能送梨
records.reason: 发音“なし”与“無し”同音，寓意“没有/消失”
records.alternative: 送苹果/草莓

records.taboo: ナシを贈ってはならない
records.reason: 「なし」の発音が「無し」と同じで、「なくなる/消える」を意味する
records.alternative: リンゴやイチゴを贈る

records.taboo: 不能送钟表
records.reason: 谐音“ちょうこく”，有“催促”“终结”意味
records.alternative: 送怀表/装饰性计时器

records.taboo: 時計を贈ってはならない
records.reason: 「ちょうこく」の語呂合わせから、「促す」「終わり」の意味合いがある
records.alternative: 懐中時計や装飾的なタイマーを贈る

records.taboo: 不能送盆栽/根菜类礼物
records.reason: 盆栽有“扎根不动”之意，根菜谐音“根腐れ”（衰败）
records.alternative: 送切花/水果礼盒

records.taboo: 鉢植えや根菜類の贈り物を贈ってはならない

records.taboo: Specific religious symbols (e.g., cross, Jesus statue)

records.alternative: 办公实用小物（桌面收纳、无线充电器）、中性配饰（如围巾，需慎重）

records.taboo: Intimate clothing or accessories (clothing other than ties or hats)

records.taboo: 探病不能送梨
records.reason: 谐音“离”（离开），寓意不吉利
records.alternative: 送苹果（寓意平安）或橘子（寓意吉利）

records.taboo: Pears should not be given when visiting the sick
records.reason: The word "pear" is a homophone for "separate" or "leave," implying a bad omen.
records.alternative: Give apples (symbolizing peace) or tangerines (symbolizing good fortune).

records.taboo: 不能送钟
records.reason: 谐音“送终”，寓意极其不吉利
records.alternative: 无（建议选择其他饰品或摆件）

records.taboo: Clocks should not be given as gifts.
records.reason: "Giving a clock" sounds like "attending a funeral," which is extremely inauspicious.
records.alternative: None (suggest choosing other accessories or decorative items).

records.taboo: 不能送单数，忌讳数字“4”
records.reason: 传统讲究好事成双；“4”谐音“死”
records.alternative: 选择双数礼物，优选数字“6”（顺）或“8”（发）

records.taboo: Odd numbers should not be given; the number "4" is taboo.
records.reason: Tradition values pairing for good things; "4" sounds like "death."
records.alternative: Choose gifts in even numbers; numbers "6" (meaning smooth) or "8" (meaning prosperity) are preferred.

records.taboo: 不能送鞋
records.reason: 谐音“邪”
records.alternative: 无（建议送其他非谐音冲突的物品）

records.taboo: Shoes should not be given as gifts.
records.reason: "Shoe" sounds like "evil."
records.alternative: None (suggest giving other items without homophonic conflicts).

records.taboo: 不能送表
records.reason: 谐音“婊”（针对女性）
records.alternative: 无（建议避免送此类谐音敏感物品给女性）

records.taboo: Watches should not be given to women.
records.reason: "Watch" sounds like a derogatory term for women.
records.alternative: None (suggest avoiding giving such homophone-sensitive items to women).

records.taboo: 别轻易送烟、酒、药
records.reason: 收的人太多记不住；送药等于咒别人生病
records.alternative: 送有新意的特产或健康食品

records.taboo: Avoid easily giving tobacco, alcohol, or medicine.
records.reason: Too many people receive them and won't remember; giving medicine implies wishing illness upon someone.
records.alternative: Give creative local specialties or healthy food.

records.taboo: 千万别送领带、项链
records.reason: 涉及私密关系，容易造成误会
records.alternative: 无（建议避免送给非亲密关系的人）

records.taboo: Never give ties or necklaces.
records.reason: These imply an intimate relationship and can easily cause misunderstandings.
records.alternative: None (suggest avoiding giving these to non-intimate people).

records.taboo: 给钱不能直溜溜拿出来
records.reason: 显得尴尬，让对方难堪
records.alternative: 用信封或红包包起来

records.taboo: Money should not be handed over directly.
records.reason: It appears awkward and embarrassing for the recipient.
records.alternative: Wrap the money in an envelope or red packet.

records.taboo: 饭桌和办公室送礼
records.reason: 求人不送礼，送礼不求人；饭点拜访不适宜
records.alternative: 选择私下、非正式场合寒暄时赠送

records.taboo: Giving gifts at dinner tables or in offices.
records.reason: Don't give gifts when asking for favors; avoid visiting during meal times.
records.alternative: Give gifts privately in informal settings.

records.taboo: 不能送鞋（尤其是小孩穿过的）
records.reason: 谐音“邪”
records.alternative: 送袜子，寓意“步步高升”，或选择其他衣物配饰

records.taboo: Shoes (especially those worn by children) should not be given.
records.reason: "Shoe" sounds like "evil."
records.alternative: Give socks, symbolizing "rising step by step," or choose other clothing accessories.

records.taboo: 喜事不能送伞
records.reason: 谐音“散”，寓意分离
records.alternative: 送寓意“圆满”的礼物，如成对的餐具或摆件

records.taboo: Umbrellas should not be given for happy occasions.
records.reason: "Umbrella" sounds like "scatter," implying separation.
records.alternative: Give gifts symbolizing completeness, such as paired tableware or decorative items.

records.taboo: 一般情况下不送菊花
records.reason: 菊花常用于祭祀或清明节，日常送礼不吉利
records.alternative: 选择寓意吉祥的花卉，如百合（百年好合）、康乃馨（温馨祝福）

records.taboo: Generally, chrysanthemums should not be given as gifts.
records.reason: They are often used in memorials or during the Tomb-Sweeping Festival, making them inauspicious for daily gifting.
records.alternative: Choose auspicious flowers, such as lilies (symbolizing a harmonious union) or carnations (symbolizing warm blessings).

records.taboo: 不能送碗筷、药盔（熬药的锅）
records.reason: 碗筷涉及个人气运，药盔不卫生且寓意病痛
records.alternative: 送高品质的厨房小家电，如空气炸锅、破壁机

records.taboo: Bowls, chopsticks, and medicine pots should not be given.
records.reason: Bowls and chopsticks relate to personal fortune; medicine pots are unhygienic and imply illness.
records.alternative: Give high-quality small kitchen appliances, such as air fryers or blenders.

records.taboo: 不能送鱼缸
records.reason: 寓意“破财”，风水上认为会送走财运
records.alternative: 送寓意吉祥的摆件，如水晶洞、招财猫

records.taboo: Fish tanks should not be given.
records.reason: It symbolizes "losing wealth"; in feng shui, it's believed to send away financial luck.
records.alternative: Give auspicious decorative items, such as crystal geodes or Maneki-neko (lucky cats).

records.taboo: 看望病人不送柿子和梨
records.reason: “柿”与“事”谐音，结合梨的“离”，寓意“事离”或不吉；梨谐音“离”
records.alternative: 送苹果（寓意平安）、橙子（寓意心想事成）或营养品

records.taboo: Persimmons and pears should not be given when visiting the sick.
records.reason: "Persimmon" sounds like "matter/event," and combined with "pear" sounding like "separate," it implies "separation from affairs" or bad luck.
records.alternative: Give apples (symbolizing peace), oranges (symbolizing wishes coming true), or nutritional supplements.

records.taboo: 给老人拜寿不送烟
records.reason: 烟有害健康，且“寿比南山”与烟雾消散相悖
records.alternative: 送高品质的茶叶、保健品或舒适的衣物（如羊绒衫）

records.taboo: Tobacco should not be given when celebrating elders' birthdays.
records.reason: Tobacco is harmful to health, and the blessing "longevity like Mount Nan" contrasts with dissipating smoke.
records.alternative: Give high-quality tea, health products, or comfortable clothing (e.g., cashmere sweaters).

records.taboo: 给领导/长辈不送茶
records.reason: 谐音“查”（调查/审查），容易引起官场或职场联想
records.alternative: 送高品质的红酒、艺术品或办公用品（如钢笔）

records.taboo: Tea should not be given to leaders or elders.
records.reason: "Tea" sounds like "investigate/scrutinize," easily evoking associations in official or workplace contexts.
records.alternative: Give high-quality red wine, artworks, or office supplies (e.g., fountain pens).

records.taboo: 不要送拐杖
records.reason: 寓意对方身体不好，需要拄拐
records.alternative: 送按摩仪、足浴盆等健康理疗产品

records.taboo: Walking canes should not be given.
records.reason: It implies the recipient is in poor health and needs support.
records.alternative: Give health therapy products like massagers or foot baths.

records.taboo: 不要送苕帚（扫帚）
records.reason: 寓意扫走好运，影响彼此关系
records.alternative: 送寓意“招财”的摆件，如金蟾、貔貅（需注意开光）

records.taboo: Brooms should not be given.
records.reason: It implies sweeping away good luck, affecting the relationship.
records.alternative: Give wealth-attracting decorative items, such as a three-legged money toad or Pixiu (a mythical creature; note that consecration may be required).

records.taboo: 神像、佛像不能送人
records.reason: 涉及宗教神圣性，随意送礼显得不敬
records.alternative: 送寓意吉祥的工艺品，如中国结、玉佩

records.taboo: Statues of deities or Buddha should not be given.
records.reason: It involves religious sanctity; casual gifting appears disrespectful.
records.alternative: Give auspicious crafts, such as Chinese knots or jade pendants.

records.taboo: 钱包不能送人
records.reason: 钱包是“钱袋子”，送人等于送走财运
records.alternative: 在钱包里放一枚硬币，象征“买”下钱包，化解禁忌

records.taboo: Wallets should not be given away.
records.reason: Wallets are "money bags"; giving them away equals giving away financial luck.
records.alternative: Place a coin inside the wallet, symbolizing "buying" the wallet to neutralize the taboo.

records.taboo: 不要送布娃娃
records.reason: 民间迷信认为容易招“小人”或不干净的东西
records.alternative: 送益智类玩具，如乐高积木、拼图或书籍

records.taboo: Dolls should not be given.
records.reason: Folk superstition believes they may attract "small-minded people" or unclean spirits.
records.alternative: Give educational toys, such as LEGO bricks, puzzles, or books.

records.taboo: 不要送来历不明的石头和古董
records.reason: 被认为“不吉利”，可能带有不好的气场
records.alternative: 送全新的、有美好寓意的工艺品或艺术品

records.taboo: Stones and antiques with unknown origins should not be given.
records.reason: They are considered "inauspicious" and may carry bad energy.
records.alternative: Give brand-new crafts or artworks with positive meanings.

records.taboo: 蜡烛不能随便送人
records.reason: 蜡烛常用于祭祀或长明灯，寓意不好
records.alternative: 送香薰机、无火香薰或高品质的精油

records.taboo: Candles should not be casually given.
records.reason: They are often used in rituals or as eternal flames, carrying bad connotations.
records.alternative: Give diffusers, flameless aromatherapy, or high-quality essential oils.

records.taboo: 避免送文玩手串、貔貅
records.reason: 物品带有个人“信息存储”或磁场，不适合转送
records.alternative: 送全新的、设计简约的饰品，或高品质的钢笔

records.taboo: Avoid giving cultural bracelet beads or Pixiu.
records.reason: Such items are believed to store personal "information" or energy and are unsuitable for regifting.
records.alternative: Give new, simply designed jewelry or high-quality fountain pens.

records.taboo: 祖传物品不要送人
records.reason: 涉及家族气运和情感，酒后尤其注意
records.alternative: 无（祖传物品本身就不适合作为礼物）

records.taboo: Family heirlooms should not be given away.
records.reason: They involve family fortune and emotions; be especially cautious after drinking alcohol.
records.alternative: None (heirlooms are inherently unsuitable as gifts).

records.taboo: 不要送猛兽类或不知寓意的挂画
records.reason: 猛兽伤人，不知寓意的画可能含有凶煞
records.alternative: 送寓意吉祥的山水画、花鸟画或书法作品

records.taboo: Paintings of fierce animals or those with unknown meanings should not be given.
records.reason: Fierce animals can harm; paintings with unknown meanings may contain evil spirits.
records.alternative: Give auspicious landscape paintings, bird-and-flower paintings, or calligraphy works.

records.taboo: 葫芦不能送人
records.reason: 葫芦谐音“福禄”，送人等于送走自家福气
records.alternative: 送寓意“多子多福”的石榴摆件或“事事如意”的柿饼礼盒

records.taboo: Gourds should not be given away.
records.reason: "Gourd" sounds like "blessings and prosperity"; giving it away means losing one's own fortune.
records.alternative: Give pomegranate figurines (symbolizing many children and blessings) or persimmon cake gift boxes (symbolizing "everything goes as wished").

records.taboo: 不能送帽子
records.reason: 俗话“愁帽子”，寓意带来忧愁
records.alternative: 送围巾、手套等保暖配饰，寓意“温暖关怀”

records.taboo: Hats should not be given.
records.reason: A saying goes "worrisome hat," implying bringing sorrow.
records.alternative: Give scarves, gloves, or other warm accessories, symbolizing "warm care."

records.taboo: 不能送被子
records.reason: 谐音“背”（背运），寓意不吉
records.alternative: 送高品质的床上四件套，寓意“安稳度日”

records.taboo: Quilts should not be given.
records.reason: "Quilt" sounds like "bad luck," implying inauspiciousness.
records.alternative: Give high-quality bedding sets, symbolizing "living a stable life."

records.taboo: 不能送杯子
records.reason: 谐音“杯”（悲），或“一辈子”悲
records.alternative: 送保温杯（寓意“温暖一辈子”），或搭配茶叶、糖果一起送

records.taboo: Cups should not be given.
records.reason: "Cup" sounds like "sorrow," or "a lifetime of sorrow."
records.alternative: Give insulated mugs (symbolizing "warmth for a lifetime"), or pair with tea and candy.

records.taboo: 送男领导烟不能单条
records.reason: 数量不吉利或显得不够大方
records.alternative: 送双数（如两条），或搭配高档打火机组成礼盒

records.taboo: Single packs of cigarettes should not be given to male leaders.
records.reason: An odd number is inauspicious or appears stingy.
records.alternative: Give even numbers (e.g., two packs), or pair with a high-end lighter as a gift set.

records.taboo: 不送牛奶、保健品、土特产
records.reason: 被视为廉价、不懂事，容易被拒绝
records.alternative: 送有品牌知名度、包装精美的高档礼品，如高端茶叶、名酒

records.taboo: Milk, health supplements, or local specialties should not be given.
records.reason: They are seen as cheap and naive, easily rejected.
records.alternative: Give well-known, beautifully packaged premium gifts, such as high-end tea or fine liquor.

records.taboo: 第一次去老丈人家带牛奶水果
records.reason: 显得廉价，被视为“外人”，被认为不懂事
records.alternative: 送茅台、华子（中华烟），确立“自己人”地位

records.taboo: Bringing milk and fruit on the first visit to the future in-laws.
records.reason: It appears cheap, makes you seem like an "outsider," and is considered naive.
records.alternative: Give Moutai, Zhonghua cigarettes (Huazi), establishing your status as "one of the family."

records.taboo: 送土特产、土鸡蛋
records.reason: 价值低且给对方造成处理麻烦（易坏、难变现）
records.alternative: 送茅台、华子、现金、黄金等“硬通货”

records.taboo: Local specialties or farm eggs should not be given.
records.reason: Low value and troublesome for the recipient (perishable, hard to monetize).
records.alternative: Give "hard currency" like Moutai, Zhonghua cigarettes, cash, or gold.

records.taboo: 送牛奶、水果
records.reason: 容易过期腐烂，被视为“垃圾”，显得没诚意
records.alternative: 送高档烟酒或现金，让对方心里踏实

records.taboo: Milk or fruit should not be given.
records.reason: They easily expire and rot, are seen as "trash," and appear insincere.
records.alternative: Give high-end tobacco, alcohol, or cash, making the recipient feel secure.

records.taboo: 送牛奶、水果
records.reason: 容易过期腐烂，被视为“垃圾”，显得没诚意
records.alternative: 送高档烟酒或现金，让对方心里踏实

records.taboo: Milk or fruit should not be given.
records.reason: They easily expire and rot, are seen as "trash," and appear insincere.
records.alternative: Give high-end tobacco, alcohol, or cash, making the recipient feel secure.

records.taboo: 送保健品
records.reason: 对方不敢吃（怕吃坏担责），容易被直接丢弃
records.alternative: 送对方已知喜好且安全的消耗品（烟酒茶）

records.taboo: Health supplements should not be given.
records.reason: Recipients dare not consume them (fearing side effects and responsibility), and they are easily discarded.
records.alternative: Give safe consumables (tobacco, alcohol, tea) that the recipient is known to like.

records.taboo: 送不知行情的花瓶、字画
records.reason: 对方无法判断价值，不敢轻易收受，心理负担重
records.alternative: 送价格透明的“硬通货”（烟酒金）

records.taboo: Vases or calligraphy with unknown market value should not be given.
records.reason: Recipients cannot judge the value, hesitate to accept, and feel a psychological burden.
records.alternative: Give price-transparent "hard currency" (tobacco, alcohol, gold).

records.taboo: 直接送钱给不熟的人
records.reason: 关系不到位，对方不敢收，显得突兀
records.alternative: 循序渐进（水果->烟酒->现金），或“借花献佛”

records.taboo: Money should not be given directly to unfamiliar people.
records.reason: The relationship is not close enough; the recipient dares not accept it, and it appears abrupt.
records.alternative: Use a gradual approach (fruits → tobacco/alcohol → cash), or use the "present from another" method.

records.taboo: 给有钱人只谈钱
records.reason: 有钱人缺的是面子和尊重，只谈钱显得俗气
records.alternative: 满足其精神需求（面子、认可），给予尊重

records.taboo: Only talking about money with wealthy people.
records.reason: What wealthy people lack is face and respect; only talking about money appears vulgar.
records.alternative: Fulfill their spiritual needs (face, recognition); show them respect.

records.taboo: 送价值模糊的礼物
records.reason: 对方不知道价格，无法衡量人情轻重
records.alternative: 送价格透明、市场公认的高价值物品

records.taboo: Gifts with ambiguous value should not be given.
records.reason: The recipient doesn't know the price and cannot gauge the weight of the favor.
records.alternative: Give price-transparent, market-recognized high-value items.

records.taboo: 探病不送柿子和梨
records.reason: 谐音“事离”（事事分离）或“离”（离开），寓意病情加重或分离
records.alternative: 送苹果（寓意平安）或橙子（寓意心想事成）

records.taboo: Persimmons and pears should not be given when visiting the sick.
records.reason: The homophones imply "separation from affairs" or "leave," suggesting worsening illness or separation.
records.alternative: Give apples (symbolizing peace) or oranges (symbolizing wishes coming true).

records.taboo: 探病不送柿子和梨
records.reason: 谐音“事离”（事事分离）或“离”（离开），寓意病情加重或分离
records.alternative: 送苹果（寓意平安）或橙子（寓意心想事成）

records.taboo: Persimmons and pears should not be given when visiting the sick.
records.reason: The homophones imply "separation from affairs" or "leave," suggesting worsening illness or separation.
records.alternative: Give apples (symbolizing peace) or oranges (symbolizing wishes coming true).

records.taboo: 祝寿、结婚不送伞和扇
records.reason: 谐音“散”，寓意离散、分手或家庭破裂，不吉利
records.alternative: 送成对的喜庆摆件、家纺或寓意“圆满”的礼物

records.taboo: Umbrellas and fans should not be given for birthdays or weddings.
records.reason: They sound like "scatter," implying separation, breakup, or family rupture, which is inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give paired festive ornaments, home textiles, or gifts symbolizing "completeness."

records.taboo: 给谨慎的领导不送茶
records.reason: 谐音“查”，容易引起官场被调查的联想，犯忌讳
records.alternative: 送烟酒、现金或土特产（硬通货）

records.taboo: Tea should not be given to cautious leaders.
records.reason: "Tea" sounds like "investigate," easily evoking associations with official scrutiny, making it taboo.
records.alternative: Give tobacco, alcohol, cash, or local specialties (hard currency).

records.taboo: 送白酒不能只送一瓶
records.reason: 违背“好事成双”的传统习俗，单数显得不吉利或吝啬
records.alternative: 送双数（如两瓶），寓意成双成对

records.taboo: Only one bottle of liquor should not be given.
records.reason: It violates the tradition of "good things come in pairs"; an odd number seems inauspicious or stingy.
records.alternative: Give even numbers (e.g., two bottles), symbolizing pairing and completeness.

records.taboo: 给男领导送烟不能只送一条
records.reason: 同样遵循“好事成双”原则，单条显得分量不足
records.alternative: 送两条或成条成箱送，显得大方得体

records.taboo: Only one pack of cigarettes should not be given to male leaders.
records.reason: The same principle of "good things come in pairs" applies; one pack seems insufficient.
records.alternative: Give two packs or whole cartons/boxes, appearing generous and proper.

records.taboo: 给女领导直接送个人物品
records.reason: 容易涉及隐私或产生误会，界限感难把握
records.alternative: 送她家里老人或小孩的东西（如儿童玩具、老人补品）

records.taboo: Personal items should not be given directly to female leaders.
records.reason: It easily involves privacy or causes misunderstandings, and boundaries are hard to define.
records.alternative: Give items for her elderly family members or children (e.g., children's toys, health supplements for the elderly).

records.category: 钟表、枯萎花卉、临期食品
records.taboo: 不送钟表、枯萎花卉、临期食品
records.reason: 谐音“送终”，关联“终结”“衰败”之意
records.alternative: 送新鲜花卉、保质期长的食品或其他喜庆礼物

records.taboo: Clocks, withered flowers, and near-expiry food should not be given.
records.reason: "Giving a clock" sounds like "attending a funeral," associated with "end" and "decline."
records.alternative: Give fresh flowers, food with a long shelf life, or other joyful gifts.

records.taboo: 孕妇忌送钟表、刀具、药品类礼物
records.reason: 钟表象征"送终"不吉利，刀具代表锋利有伤害风险，药品可能影响胎儿健康
records.alternative: 送孕妇营养品、舒适衣物、婴儿用品、孕期书籍

records.taboo: Avoid giving clocks, knives, and medical products to pregnant women.
records.reason: Clocks symbolize "attending a funeral" which is inauspicious; knives imply sharpness and injury risk; medicines may affect fetal health.
records.alternative: Give maternal nutrition products, comfortable clothing, baby items, and pregnancy books.

records.taboo: 给生意人忌送雨伞、扇子、空钱包
records.reason: 雨伞象征"散财"，扇子有"扇走财运"之意，空钱包寓意"财运空空"
records.alternative: 送招财摆件、优质茶具、商务钢笔、寓意吉祥的字画

records.taboo: Avoid giving umbrellas, fans, and empty wallets to business people.
records.reason: Umbrellas symbolize "losing wealth"; fans imply "fanning away fortune"; empty wallets mean "empty fortune."
records.alternative: Give wealth-attracting ornaments, high-quality tea sets, business fountain pens, and calligraphy/paintings with auspicious meanings.

records.taboo: 给老人忌送钟表、拐杖（非必需时）、寿衣相关物品
records.reason: 钟表象征"倒计时"不吉利，非必需送拐杖暗示老人行动不便，寿衣相关物品过于敏感
records.alternative: 送保健器材、保暖用品、营养品、怀旧书籍

records.taboo: Avoid giving clocks, canes (when unnecessary), and funeral clothing-related items to the elderly.
records.reason: Clocks symbolize a "countdown" which is inauspicious; giving a cane when unnecessary implies mobility issues; funeral clothing items are too sensitive.
records.alternative: Give health care equipment, warm clothing, nutrition products, and nostalgic books.

records.taboo: 端午节忌送粽子数量为单数，忌送白色粽子
records.reason: 端午节讲究成双成对，单数不吉利；白色粽子常用于祭祀，不适合作为礼物
records.alternative: 送双数包装的粽子、艾草制品、香囊、雄黄酒（适量）

records.taboo: Avoid giving zongzi in odd numbers and white-colored zongzi during the Dragon Boat Festival.
records.reason: The festival emphasizes pairing; odd numbers are inauspicious. White zongzi are often used for sacrifices and are unsuitable as gifts.
records.alternative: Give zongzi in even-numbered packages, mugwort products, sachets, and realgar wine (in moderation).

records.taboo: 清明节忌送菊花、白色花束、食品类礼物
records.reason: 菊花和白色花束用于祭祀扫墓，食品类礼物容易让人联想到祭品，不适合节日赠送
records.alternative: 送绿色植物、实用生活用品、健康保健品

records.taboo: Avoid giving chrysanthemums, white bouquets, and food gifts during the Qingming Festival.
records.reason: Chrysanthemums and white bouquets are used for tomb-sweeping; food gifts easily evoke associations with sacrificial offerings.
records.alternative: Give green plants, practical daily necessities, and health care products.

records.taboo: 元宵节忌送单数元宵，忌送无馅元宵
records.reason: 元宵节讲究团圆美满，单数不吉利；无馅元宵象征"无福无禄"，寓意不好
records.alternative: 送双数包装元宵、灯笼、吉祥饰品、特色糕点

records.taboo: Avoid giving yuanxiao in odd numbers and yuanxiao without fillings during the Lantern Festival.
records.reason: The festival emphasizes reunion and completeness; odd numbers are inauspicious. Yuanxiao without fillings symbolize "no fortune or blessings."
records.alternative: Give yuanxiao in even-numbered packages, lanterns, auspicious ornaments, and specialty pastries.

records.taboo: 南方地区忌送粽子（非端午期间）、年糕给生意人
records.reason: 粽子在南方有"终结"之意，非端午送粽子不吉利；年糕谐音"年高"，给生意人送有"年年高升"压力感
records.alternative: 送茶叶、海鲜礼盒、特色水果、工艺品

records.taboo: Avoid giving zongzi (outside the Dragon Boat Festival) and rice cakes to business people in southern China.
records.reason: Zongzi can imply "end" in the south; giving them outside the festival is inauspicious. "Rice cake" sounds like "yearly promotion," creating pressure.
records.alternative: Give tea, seafood gift boxes, specialty fruits, and handicrafts.

records.taboo: 北方地区忌送钟表、手帕给长辈，忌送单数礼品
records.reason: 北方对钟表"送终"谐音更敏感，手帕有"送别离"之意，单数礼品不符合北方讲究圆满的传统
records.alternative: 送食品礼盒、保暖用品、健康器材、实用家居

records.taboo: Avoid giving clocks and handkerchiefs to elders, and odd-numbered gifts in northern China.
records.reason: Northern China is more sensitive to the homophone "giving a clock = attending a funeral"; handkerchiefs imply "sending off." Odd numbers conflict with the tradition of completeness.
records.alternative: Give food gift boxes, warm clothing, health equipment, and practical home items.

records.taboo: 送电子产品忌送二手产品，忌送功能复杂的老年机给老人
records.reason: 二手电子产品有"用过"的嫌弃感，功能复杂的老年机让老人难以操作，显得不用心
records.alternative: 送全新正品、操作简单的智能设备、实用配件

records.taboo: Avoid giving second-hand electronic products and complex-function elderly phones to the elderly.
records.reason: Second-hand electronics feel "used"; complex phones are hard for the elderly to operate, showing a lack of care.
records.alternative: Give brand-new authentic products, easy-to-operate smart devices, and practical accessories.

records.taboo: 送奢侈品忌送仿冒品，忌送超出对方消费水平的奢侈品
records.reason: 仿冒品显得不尊重且有欺骗嫌疑，超出消费水平的奢侈品让对方有心理压力和回礼负担
records.alternative: 送正品轻奢品、实用奢侈品配件、品牌文具

records.taboo: Avoid giving counterfeit luxury goods and luxury items beyond the recipient's consumption level.
records.reason: Counterfeits show disrespect and suggest deception. Items beyond their level create psychological pressure and a burden to reciprocate.
records.alternative: Give authentic light luxury products, practical luxury accessories, and brand-name stationery.

records.taboo: 初次商务合作忌送过于昂贵礼品，忌送个人化用品
records.reason: 昂贵礼品易被误解为贿赂，个人化用品超出商务边界，显得不专业
records.alternative: 送公司特色产品、实用办公用品、地方特产礼盒

records.taboo: Avoid giving overly expensive gifts and personalized items for a first business cooperation.
records.reason: Expensive gifts are easily misunderstood as bribes; personalized items exceed business boundaries and seem unprofessional.
records.alternative: Give company-specific products, practical office supplies, and local specialty gift boxes.

records.taboo: 给政府客户忌送任何现金、购物卡、贵重礼品
records.reason: 违反廉洁从政规定，可能给对方带来纪律风险，影响双方正常合作
records.alternative: 送书籍、纪念徽章、定制笔记本、地方文化产品

records.taboo: Avoid giving any cash, shopping cards, or valuable gifts to government clients.
records.reason: This violates regulations on clean governance, may bring disciplinary risks to the recipient, and affect normal cooperation.
records.alternative: Give books, commemorative badges, customized notebooks, and local cultural products.

records.taboo: 探望病人不送粽子
records.reason: 谐音“终”，有生命终结的不祥寓意，尤其对重病患者不吉利
records.alternative: 送营养粥品、蛋白粉等实用营养品，或鲜花（避开白色）

records.taboo: Zongzi (glutinous rice dumplings) should not be given when visiting patients.
records.reason: The homophone sounds like "end," implying the end of life, which is especially inauspicious for seriously ill patients.
records.alternative: Give nutritious porridge, protein powder, or other practical nutritional supplements, or flowers (avoid white).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送冬瓜
records.reason: “冬瓜”谐音“冬寡”，寓意新娘会守寡，对婚姻不吉利
records.alternative: 送红枣、桂圆、莲子（早生贵子），或喜糖礼盒

records.taboo: Winter melon should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: "Winter melon" sounds like "winter widow," implying the bride will be widowed, which is inauspicious for the marriage.
records.alternative: Give red dates, longans, lotus seeds (symbolizing having children soon), or wedding candy gift boxes.

records.taboo: 春节不送咸鱼
records.reason: “咸鱼”象征生活困苦、贫穷，春节讲究吉祥富贵
records.alternative: 送腊肉、香肠、坚果礼盒等寓意富足的食品

records.taboo: Salted fish should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: "Salted fish" symbolizes a life of hardship and poverty, while the Spring Festival emphasizes auspiciousness and wealth.
records.alternative: Give preserved meat, sausages, nut gift boxes, or other foods symbolizing abundance.

records.taboo: 给长辈不送罐头食品
records.reason: “罐头”有封闭、不新鲜之意，暗示长辈思想陈旧或生活封闭
records.alternative: 送新鲜水果篮、有机蔬菜礼盒或传统糕点

records.taboo: Canned food should not be given to elders.
records.reason: "Canned" implies being closed off and not fresh, suggesting that the elder has outdated thinking or a closed life.
records.alternative: Give fresh fruit baskets, organic vegetable gift boxes, or traditional pastries.

records.taboo: 探病不送荔枝
records.reason: “荔枝”谐音“离枝”，有分离、离世的不祥寓意
records.alternative: 送苹果（平安）、橙子（成功）、葡萄（多子多福）

records.taboo: Lychees should not be given when visiting the sick.
records.reason: "Lychee" sounds like "leave the branch," implying separation or death, an inauspicious meaning.
records.alternative: Give apples (symbolizing peace), oranges (symbolizing success), or grapes (symbolizing many blessings).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送粉丝
records.reason: “粉丝”细长如丝，象征生活琐碎缠绕、麻烦不断
records.alternative: 送米（衣食无忧）、油（生活富足）、面粉（白面书生，寓意吉祥）

records.taboo: Vermicelli should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Vermicelli is thin and stringy, symbolizing a life tangled in trivial matters and constant trouble.
records.alternative: Give rice (symbolizing worry-free衣食), oil (symbolizing a prosperous life), or flour (symbolizing good fortune).

records.taboo: 给生意人不送梨
records.reason: “梨”谐音“离”，有生意离散、客户流失的不祥寓意
records.alternative: 送苹果（平安经营）、橙子（事业成功）、柚子（保佑平安）

records.taboo: Pears should not be given to business people.
records.reason: "Pear" sounds like "separate," implying the loss of business and customers, which is inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give apples (symbolizing safe business operations), oranges (symbolizing career success), or pomelos (symbolizing protection and peace).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送蛋糕
records.reason: 传统中式婚礼讲究完整、圆满，蛋糕需要切开，有“分离”之意
records.alternative: 送龙凤喜饼、年糕（步步高升）、汤圆（团团圆圆）

records.taboo: Cakes should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Traditional Chinese weddings value wholeness and completeness; a cake needs to be cut, implying "separation."
records.alternative: Give dragon and phoenix wedding cakes, rice cakes (symbolizing rising step by step), or glutinous rice balls (symbolizing reunion).

records.taboo: 春节不送饺子
records.reason: 饺子需要水煮，有“煮”（煎熬）的寓意，春节讲究顺利
records.alternative: 送年糕（年年高）、馒头（蒸蒸日上）、汤圆（团团圆圆）

records.taboo: Dumplings should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Dumplings need to be boiled, which has a connotation of "suffering" (as in "boiling"), while the Spring Festival emphasizes smoothness.
records.alternative: Give rice cakes (symbolizing yearly improvement), steamed buns (symbolizing rising prosperity), or glutinous rice balls (symbolizing reunion).

records.taboo: 给考生不送西瓜
records.reason: 西瓜需要切开分食，有“分散精力”“考试分心”的寓意
records.alternative: 送苹果（平安考试）、橙子（成功上岸）、核桃（健脑益智）

records.taboo: Watermelons should not be given to students taking exams.
records.reason: Watermelons need to be cut and shared, implying "distracted energy" or "losing focus during the exam."
records.alternative: Give apples (symbolizing peaceful exams), oranges (symbolizing success), or walnuts (symbolizing brain health).

records.taboo: 探病不送芒果
records.reason: “芒果”谐音“忙过”，有“忙完这一阵就过去了”的不祥暗示
records.alternative: 送草莓（甜蜜康复）、樱桃（吉祥如意）、猕猴桃（健康长寿）

records.taboo: Mangoes should not be given when visiting the sick.
records.reason: "Mango" sounds like "busy finishing," with the inauspicious implication of "being finished after this busy period."
records.alternative: Give strawberries (symbolizing sweet recovery), cherries (symbolizing good luck), or kiwis (symbolizing health and longevity).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送面条
records.reason: 面条细长，有“日子过得细长”（不富裕）的寓意
records.alternative: 送大米（衣食无忧）、食用油（生活富足）、面粉（白面书生）

records.taboo: Noodles should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Noodles are long and thin, implying that life will be "long and thin" (not wealthy).
records.alternative: Give rice (symbolizing worry-free衣食), cooking oil (symbolizing a prosperous life), or flour (symbolizing good fortune).

records.taboo: 给新婚夫妇不送梨
records.reason: “梨”谐音“离”，有婚姻分离、离婚的不祥寓意
records.alternative: 送红枣（早生贵子）、桂圆（圆满团圆）、莲子（连生贵子）

records.taboo: Pears should not be given to newlyweds.
records.reason: "Pear" sounds like "separate," implying separation or divorce, which is inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give red dates (symbolizing having children soon), longans (symbolizing complete reunion), or lotus seeds (symbolizing having children in succession).

records.taboo: 春节不送豆腐
records.reason: 豆腐颜色洁白，有丧葬用白色的联想，春节讲究喜庆
records.alternative: 送腐竹（富足）、木耳（发财）、香菇（吉祥如意）

records.taboo: Tofu should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Tofu is white, evoking associations with the white color used in funerals, while the Spring Festival emphasizes festivity.
records.alternative: Give dried bean curd sticks (symbolizing wealth), wood ear fungus (symbolizing making a fortune), or shiitake mushrooms (symbolizing good luck).

records.taboo: 给老人不送糖果
records.reason: 老人多有血糖问题，送糖果可能暗示老人身体不健康，且易蛀牙
records.alternative: 送无糖糕点、坚果礼盒、保健营养品

records.taboo: Candy should not be given to the elderly.
records.reason: Many elderly people have blood sugar issues; giving candy might imply poor health and also risks causing tooth decay.
records.alternative: Give sugar-free pastries, nut gift boxes, or health supplements.

records.taboo: 探病不送香蕉
records.reason: 香蕉需要剥皮，有“剥掉健康”的不祥联想，且熟香蕉易腐烂
records.alternative: 送苹果（平安）、橙子（成功康复）、葡萄（多福多寿）

records.taboo: Bananas should not be given when visiting the sick.
records.reason: Bananas need to be peeled, evoking the inauspicious notion of "peeling away health." Ripe bananas also rot easily.
records.alternative: Give apples (symbolizing peace), oranges (symbolizing successful recovery), or grapes (symbolizing many blessings and longevity).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送咸菜
records.reason: 咸菜味道咸，有“生活过得咸”（不幸福）的寓意，且腌制食品不新鲜
records.alternative: 送新鲜蔬菜、水果篮、粮油礼盒

records.taboo: Pickled vegetables should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: They are salty, implying a "salty life" (not happy), and preserved foods are not fresh.
records.alternative: Give fresh vegetables, fruit baskets, or gift boxes of grain and oil.

records.taboo: 给生意人不送螃蟹
records.reason: 螃蟹横行，有“横行霸道”的负面联想，不利于商业形象
records.alternative: 送龙虾（龙马精神）、鲍鱼（富贵吉祥）、海参（健康长寿）

records.taboo: Crabs should not be given to business people.
records.reason: Crabs walk sideways, evoking negative associations of "arrogance and tyranny," which is unfavorable for a business image.
records.alternative: Give lobsters (symbolizing vigor), abalone (symbolizing wealth and good fortune), or sea cucumbers (symbolizing health and longevity).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送花生
records.reason: 花生有“花落生”的谐音，有婚姻不长久的不祥寓意
records.alternative: 送红枣（早生贵子）、桂圆（圆满团圆）、瓜子（多子多福）

records.taboo: Peanuts should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: "Peanut" has a homophone suggesting "flowers fall and life ends," implying the marriage may not last.
records.alternative: Give red dates (symbolizing having children soon), longans (symbolizing complete reunion), or melon seeds (symbolizing many children and blessings).

records.taboo: 春节不送糖果
records.reason: 糖果易融化，有“甜蜜短暂”的寓意，春节讲究长久吉祥
records.alternative: 送坚果礼盒、干果礼盒、巧克力礼盒（包装精美）

records.taboo: Candy should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Candy melts easily, implying that "sweetness is short-lived," while the Spring Festival emphasizes lasting good fortune.
records.alternative: Give nut gift boxes, dried fruit gift boxes, or beautifully packaged chocolate gift boxes.

records.taboo: 不送手帕给朋友
records.reason: 手帕用于擦眼泪，有“分离哭泣”的寓意，尤其不适合送给恋人
records.alternative: 送围巾（温暖陪伴）、手套（贴心呵护）、帽子（时尚保暖）

records.taboo: Handkerchiefs should not be given to friends.
records.reason: Handkerchiefs are used to wipe tears, implying "separation and sorrow," making them especially unsuitable for lovers.
records.alternative: Give scarves (symbolizing warm companionship), gloves (symbolizing thoughtful care), or hats (symbolizing fashion and warmth).

records.taboo: 不送梳子给异性朋友
records.reason: “梳”谐音“输”，有“输给对方”的寓意，且梳子贴身，易引起误会
records.alternative: 送钢笔（文化气质）、笔记本（实用记录）、书签（书香气息）

records.taboo: Combs should not be given to friends of the opposite sex.
records.reason: "Comb" sounds like "lose," implying "losing to the other person." Combs are also personal items, easily causing misunderstandings.
records.alternative: Give fountain pens (symbolizing cultural taste), notebooks (practical for recording), or bookmarks (symbolizing a scholarly atmosphere).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送镜子
records.reason: 镜子易破碎，有“破镜难圆”的寓意，且镜子反射，有“反射福气”之意
records.alternative: 送装饰画（美化家居）、绿植（生机勃勃）、台灯（光明吉祥）

records.taboo: Mirrors should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Mirrors break easily, implying "a broken mirror cannot be made whole," and they reflect, which is thought to "reflect away good fortune."
records.alternative: Give decorative paintings (beautifying the home), green plants (symbolizing vitality), or table lamps (symbolizing brightness and good fortune).

records.taboo: 不送毛巾给病人
records.reason: 毛巾用于清洁，有“擦拭病痛”的联想，但也易让人联想到疾病传播
records.alternative: 送保温杯（温暖关怀）、加湿器（改善环境）、舒适靠垫（缓解不适）

records.taboo: Towels should not be given to patients.
records.reason: While used for cleaning and associated with "wiping away illness," they can also evoke thoughts of disease transmission.
records.alternative: Give insulated cups (symbolizing warm care), humidifiers (improving the environment), or comfortable cushions (relieving discomfort).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送剪刀
records.reason: 剪刀有切割之意，有“剪断姻缘”的不祥寓意，对婚姻不吉利
records.alternative: 送刀具套装（需用红布包裹，寓意红火）、餐具套装（丰衣足食）

records.taboo: Scissors should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Scissors cut, implying "cutting the marital bond," which is extremely inauspicious for the marriage.
records.alternative: Give knife sets (should be wrapped in red cloth, symbolizing prosperity) or tableware sets (symbolizing abundant food and clothing).

records.taboo: 不送雨伞给朋友
records.reason: “伞”谐音“散”，有“分散、离别”的寓意，尤其不适合送给恋人
records.alternative: 送雨衣（实用防护）、防晒衣（贴心呵护）、遮阳帽（时尚防晒）

records.taboo: Umbrellas should not be given to friends.
records.reason: "Umbrella" sounds like "scatter," implying separation or parting, making it especially unsuitable for lovers.
records.alternative: Give raincoats (practical protection), sun-protective clothing (thoughtful care), or sun hats (stylish sun protection).

records.taboo: 春节不送洗涤用品
records.reason: 洗涤用品用于清洁污垢，有“洗掉福气”的寓意，春节讲究保留福气
records.alternative: 送家居装饰品、床上用品、厨房用品（非清洁类）

records.taboo: Cleaning products should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: They are used to clean dirt, implying "washing away good fortune," while the Spring Festival emphasizes retaining good fortune.
records.alternative: Give home decorations, bedding, or kitchen supplies (non-cleaning items).

records.taboo: 不送牙刷给他人
records.reason: 牙刷过于贴身私密，送牙刷有侵犯隐私之嫌，且不卫生
records.alternative: 送牙膏（实用卫生）、漱口水（健康护理）、牙线（细致关怀）

records.taboo: Toothbrushes should not be given to others.
records.reason: Toothbrushes are too personal and private; giving one can be seen as an invasion of privacy and is unhygienic.
records.alternative: Give toothpaste (practical and hygienic), mouthwash (health care), or dental floss (detailed care).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送钟表
records.reason: “钟”谐音“终”，有“生命终结”的不祥寓意，对新家不吉利
records.alternative: 送挂历（记录时光）、台灯（光明吉祥）、装饰画（美化环境）

records.taboo: Clocks should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: "Clock" sounds like "end," implying the end of life, which is inauspicious for a new home.
records.alternative: Give wall calendars (marking time), table lamps (symbolizing brightness and good fortune), or decorative paintings (beautifying the environment).

records.taboo: 不送拖鞋给他人
records.reason: 拖鞋是家居穿着，过于随意，送拖鞋有“让对方放松警惕”的负面联想
records.alternative: 送皮鞋（正式得体）、运动鞋（健康活力）、休闲鞋（舒适时尚）

records.taboo: Slippers should not be given to others.
records.reason: Slippers are casual home wear; giving them evokes the negative idea of "causing the other person to let their guard down."
records.alternative: Give leather shoes (formal and appropriate), sports shoes (healthy and energetic), or casual shoes (comfortable and fashionable).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送杯子
records.reason: “杯”谐音“悲”，有“悲伤、悲痛”的寓意，对婚姻不吉利
records.alternative: 送碗碟套装（丰衣足食）、茶具套装（品味生活）、餐具套装（幸福美满）

records.taboo: Cups should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: "Cup" sounds like "sorrow," implying grief and pain, which is inauspicious for the marriage.
records.alternative: Give bowl and plate sets (symbolizing abundant food), tea sets (symbolizing a tasteful life), or tableware sets (symbolizing happiness and completeness).

records.taboo: 不送枕头给朋友
records.reason: 枕头与睡眠相关，送枕头有“让对方睡不好”的负面联想，且过于私密
records.alternative: 送靠垫（舒适支撑）、毯子（温暖陪伴）、床单（实用家居）

records.taboo: Pillows should not be given to friends.
records.reason: Pillows are related to sleep; giving one evokes the negative idea of "causing the other person to sleep poorly," and they are also too personal.
records.alternative: Give cushions (comfortable support), blankets (warm companionship), or bed sheets (practical home items).

records.taboo: 春节不送刀具
records.reason: 刀具有锋利之意，有“伤人、惹祸”的寓意，春节讲究和平吉祥
records.alternative: 送筷子（快快乐乐）、勺子（招财进宝）、叉子（西餐礼仪）

records.taboo: Knives should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Knives are sharp, implying "hurting people or causing trouble," while the Spring Festival emphasizes peace and good fortune.
records.alternative: Give chopsticks (symbolizing happiness), spoons (symbolizing attracting wealth), or forks (for Western dining etiquette).

records.taboo: 不送肥皂给他人
records.reason: 肥皂用于清洁，有“嫌弃对方不干净”的负面暗示，不礼貌
records.alternative: 送香皂礼盒（芳香宜人）、护手霜（贴心呵护）、身体乳（滋润护理）

records.taboo: Soap should not be given to others.
records.reason: Soap is used for cleaning, implying the negative suggestion that the recipient is "dirty," which is impolite.
records.alternative: Give gift boxes of scented soap (pleasant fragrance), hand cream (thoughtful care), or body lotion (moisturizing care).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送花瓶
records.reason: “瓶”谐音“贫”，有“贫穷、贫困”的寓意，对新家不吉利
records.alternative: 送绿植盆栽（生机勃勃）、装饰画（艺术氛围）、摆件（吉祥寓意）

records.taboo: Vases should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: "Vase" sounds like "poverty," implying a lack of wealth, which is inauspicious for a new home.
records.alternative: Give potted green plants (symbolizing vitality), decorative paintings (artistic atmosphere), or ornaments (auspicious meanings).

records.taboo: 不送指甲刀给他人
records.reason: 指甲刀有修剪之意，有“修剪关系”的负面联想，易引起误会
records.alternative: 送指甲护理套装（需精美包装）、手部护理礼盒、美容工具套装

records.taboo: Nail clippers should not be given to others.
records.reason: They are for trimming, evoking the negative idea of "trimming the relationship," which can easily cause misunderstandings.
records.alternative: Give nail care sets (should be beautifully packaged), hand care gift boxes, or beauty tool sets.

records.taboo: 婚礼不送镜子
records.reason: 镜子易破碎，有“破镜难圆”的寓意，对婚姻长久不吉利
records.alternative: 送化妆盒（美丽相伴）、首饰盒（珍藏幸福）、装饰画（美化家居）

records.taboo: Mirrors should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Mirrors break easily, implying "a broken mirror cannot be made whole," which is inauspicious for the longevity of the marriage.
records.alternative: Give makeup boxes (symbolizing beauty and companionship), jewelry boxes (symbolizing treasured happiness), or decorative paintings (beautifying the home).

records.taboo: 不送垃圾桶给乔迁者
records.reason: 垃圾桶用于装垃圾，有“接收垃圾”的寓意，对新家不吉利
records.alternative: 送收纳盒（整洁有序）、储物箱（收纳幸福）、整理箱（规划生活）

records.taboo: Trash cans should not be given to those moving to a new home.
records.reason: They are used for garbage, implying "receiving garbage," which is inauspicious for a new home.
records.alternative: Give storage boxes (symbolizing neatness and order), storage chests (symbolizing storing happiness), or organizing bins (symbolizing planning life).

records.taboo: 春节不送袜子
records.reason: 袜子在脚下，有“踩在脚下”的寓意，春节讲究步步高升
records.alternative: 送围巾（温暖围绕）、手套（贴心呵护）、帽子（高高在上）

records.taboo: Socks should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Socks are worn on the feet, implying being "trampled underfoot," while the Spring Festival emphasizes rising step by step.
records.alternative: Give scarves (symbolizing warm companionship), gloves (symbolizing thoughtful care), or hats (symbolizing being held in high regard).

records.taboo: 不送毛巾给恋人
records.reason: 毛巾易脏，有“感情变脏”的负面联想，且过于日常缺乏浪漫
records.alternative: 送项链（心心相印）、手链（永结同心）、戒指（永恒承诺）

records.taboo: Towels should not be given to lovers.
records.reason: Towels get dirty easily, evoking the negative idea of the "relationship becoming dirty." They are also too mundane and lack romance.
records.alternative: Give necklaces (symbolizing mutual affection), bracelets (symbolizing eternal unity), or rings (symbolizing an eternal promise).

records.taboo: 不送白色衣服给长辈
records.reason: 白色是丧葬用色，送白色衣服有“诅咒长辈”的不祥寓意
records.alternative: 送红色（吉祥喜庆）、蓝色（沉稳大气）、紫色（尊贵典雅）衣服

records.taboo: White clothes should not be given to elders.
records.reason: White is the color of mourning; giving white clothes carries the inauspicious implication of "cursing the elder."
records.alternative: Give clothes in red (auspicious and festive), blue (calm and dignified), or purple (noble and elegant).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送黑色礼服
records.reason: 黑色象征严肃、哀悼，与婚礼的喜庆氛围不符，不吉利
records.alternative: 送红色礼服（喜庆吉祥）、粉色礼服（浪漫温馨）、金色礼服（富贵华丽）

records.taboo: Black formal wear should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Black symbolizes seriousness and mourning, which clashes with the festive atmosphere of a wedding and is considered inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give red formal wear (auspicious and festive), pink formal wear (romantic and warm), or gold formal wear (wealthy and magnificent).

records.taboo: 不送旧衣服给他人
records.reason: 旧衣服有“二手”之意，有“嫌弃对方”“不尊重对方”的负面暗示
records.alternative: 送新衣服（全新祝福）、新鞋子（步步高升）、新配饰（时尚点缀）

records.taboo: Used clothes should not be given to others.
records.reason: Used clothes imply "second-hand," carrying the negative suggestion of "looking down on" or "disrespecting" the recipient.
records.alternative: Give new clothes (brand-new blessings), new shoes (symbolizing rising step by step), or new accessories (fashionable touches).

records.taboo: 春节不送绿色衣服
records.reason: “绿”谐音“禄”，但绿色也有“戴绿帽”的负面联想，春节需谨慎
records.alternative: 送红色（喜庆吉祥）、黄色（富贵尊贵）、橙色（温暖活力）衣服

records.taboo: Green clothes should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: While "green" sounds like "official's salary," it also has the negative connotation of "being cuckolded," so caution is needed.
records.alternative: Give clothes in red (auspicious and festive), yellow (wealthy and noble), or orange (warm and energetic).

records.taboo: 不送内衣给异性朋友
records.reason: 内衣过于私密，送内衣易引起误会，有“骚扰”之嫌，不礼貌
records.alternative: 送外套（大方得体）、衬衫（正式优雅）、毛衣（温暖舒适）

records.taboo: Underwear should not be given to friends of the opposite sex.
records.reason: Underwear is too personal; giving it can easily cause misunderstandings and be seen as harassment, which is impolite.
records.alternative: Give coats (dignified and appropriate), shirts (formal and elegant), or sweaters (warm and comfortable).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送睡衣
records.reason: 睡衣是家居穿着，过于随意，有“让对方放松警惕”的负面联想
records.alternative: 送家居服套装（舒适得体）、外套（大方实用）、围巾（温暖陪伴）

records.taboo: Pajamas should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Pajamas are casual home wear; giving them evokes the negative idea of "causing the other person to let their guard down."
records.alternative: Give loungewear sets (comfortable and decent), coats (generous and practical), or scarves (symbolizing warm companionship).

records.taboo: 不送破洞牛仔裤给长辈
records.reason: 破洞牛仔裤有“破烂”之意，有“嫌弃长辈”“不尊重长辈”的暗示
records.alternative: 送直筒牛仔裤（大方得体）、休闲裤（舒适实用）、西裤（正式优雅）

records.taboo: Ripped jeans should not be given to elders.
records.reason: Ripped jeans imply "tattered," suggesting "looking down on" or "disrespecting" the elder.
records.alternative: Give straight-leg jeans (dignified and appropriate), casual pants (comfortable and practical), or dress pants (formal and elegant).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送吊带裙
records.reason: 吊带裙过于暴露，不符合传统婚礼的庄重氛围，易引起不适
records.alternative: 送连衣裙（优雅大方）、旗袍（传统美丽）、套装（正式得体）

records.taboo: Spaghetti strap dresses should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: They are too revealing and do not align with the solemn atmosphere of a traditional wedding, easily causing discomfort.
records.alternative: Give dresses (elegant and graceful), cheongsams (traditional beauty), or suits (formal and appropriate).

records.taboo: 不送拖鞋给长辈
records.reason: 拖鞋是家居穿着，过于随意，送拖鞋有“不尊重长辈”的暗示
records.alternative: 送皮鞋（正式得体）、布鞋（舒适健康）、休闲鞋（大方实用）

records.taboo: Slippers should not be given to elders.
records.reason: Slippers are casual home wear; giving them implies a lack of respect for the elder.
records.alternative: Give leather shoes (formal and appropriate), cloth shoes (comfortable and healthy), or casual shoes (generous and practical).

records.taboo: 春节不送短裤
records.reason: 短裤有“短”之意，有“寿命短”“福气短”的不祥寓意，春节讲究长久
records.alternative: 送长裤（长长久久）、外套（温暖陪伴）、毛衣（舒适保暖）

records.taboo: Shorts should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Shorts imply "shortness," carrying the inauspicious meanings of "short life" or "short-lived blessings," while the festival emphasizes longevity.
records.alternative: Give long pants (symbolizing longevity), coats (warm companionship), or sweaters (comfortable and warm).

records.taboo: 不送紧身衣给他人

records.alternative: 送宽松上衣（舒适自在）、休闲裤（大方实用）、外套（得体大方）

records.taboo: Tight-fitting clothes should not be given to others.
records.reason: They are form-fitting and can draw attention to body shape issues, implying the impolite suggestion of "commenting on the other's figure."
records.alternative: Give loose tops (comfortable and relaxed), casual pants (generous and practical), or coats (dignified and appropriate).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送帽子
records.reason: “帽”谐音“冒”，有“冒失、冒险”的寓意，对新家安定不吉利
records.alternative: 送围巾（温暖围绕）、手套（贴心呵护）、外套（大方实用）

records.taboo: Hats should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: "Hat" sounds like "rash," implying rashness or risk-taking, which is inauspicious for the stability of a new home.
records.alternative: Give scarves (symbolizing warm companionship), gloves (symbolizing thoughtful care), or coats (generous and practical).

records.taboo: 不送袜子给恋人
records.reason: 袜子在脚下，有“踩在脚下”的负面联想，不利于恋爱关系
records.alternative: 送围巾（温暖围绕）、手链（永结同心）、项链（心心相印）

records.taboo: Socks should not be given to lovers.
records.reason: Socks are worn on the feet, evoking the negative idea of being "trampled underfoot," which is detrimental to a romantic relationship.
records.alternative: Give scarves (symbolizing warm companionship), bracelets (symbolizing eternal unity), or necklaces (symbolizing mutual affection).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送白色婚纱（除非是新娘本人）

records.taboo: White wedding dresses should not be given as gifts (unless the bride herself is receiving it).

records.taboo: 不送泳衣给普通朋友

records.taboo: Swimsuits should not be given to casual friends.

records.alternative: Give T-shirts (casual and comfortable), shorts (generous and practical), or coats (dignified and appropriate).

records.taboo: 春节不送黑色衣服
records.reason: 黑色象征哀悼、严肃，与春节的喜庆氛围不符，不吉利
records.alternative: 送红色（喜庆吉祥）、黄色（富贵尊贵）、粉色（温馨浪漫）衣服

records.taboo: Black clothes should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Black symbolizes mourning and seriousness, which clashes with the festive atmosphere of the Spring Festival and is inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give clothes in red (auspicious and festive), yellow (wealthy and noble), or pink (warm and romantic).

records.taboo: 不送领带给非亲密关系的男性

records.taboo: Ties should not be given to men with whom you are not close.
records.reason: A tie symbolizes restraint, evoking the negative idea of "restricting the other person." It is also formal and can cause pressure.
records.alternative: Give fountain pens (symbolizing cultural taste), lighters (practical and generous), or wallets (symbolizing attracting wealth).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送腰带

records.taboo: Belts should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Belts are used to bind; they imply "binding good fortune," which is inauspicious for the development of a new home.
records.alternative: Give scarves (symbolizing warm companionship), gloves (symbolizing thoughtful care), or coats (generous and practical).

records.taboo: 不送高跟鞋给女性朋友
