records.taboo: High heels should not be given to female friends.
records.reason: They can draw attention to height, implying the impolite suggestion of "commenting on her stature."
records.alternative: Give flats (comfortable and healthy), sports shoes (healthy and energetic), or casual shoes (generous and practical).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送运动服
records.reason: 运动服过于休闲，不符合婚礼的庄重氛围，显得不重视
records.alternative: 送礼服（正式优雅）、套装（得体大方）、旗袍（传统美丽）

records.taboo: Sportswear should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: It is too casual and does not match the solemn atmosphere of a wedding, appearing disrespectful.
records.alternative: Give formal wear (formal and elegant), suits (dignified and appropriate), or cheongsams (traditional beauty).

records.taboo: 不送戒指给非恋人关系的异性
records.reason: 戒指象征婚姻承诺，送戒指给非恋人易引起严重误会，不礼貌
records.alternative: 送手链（友谊长久）、项链（美丽点缀）、耳环（时尚装饰）

records.taboo: Rings should not be given to members of the opposite sex who are not a romantic partner.
records.reason: Rings symbolize a marriage commitment; giving one to someone outside a romantic relationship can cause serious misunderstandings and is impolite.
records.alternative: Give bracelets (symbolizing lasting friendship), necklaces (beautiful accents), or earrings (fashionable decorations).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送水晶饰品
records.reason: 水晶易破碎，有“感情破碎”的不祥寓意，对婚姻长久不吉利
records.alternative: 送黄金饰品（富贵吉祥）、玉石饰品（平安健康）、珍珠饰品（纯洁美丽）

records.taboo: Crystal jewelry should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Crystal breaks easily, implying "shattered feelings," which is inauspicious for the longevity of the marriage.
records.alternative: Give gold jewelry (symbolizing wealth and good fortune), jade jewelry (symbolizing peace and health), or pearl jewelry (symbolizing purity and beauty).

records.taboo: 不送手表给长辈
records.reason: “表”谐音“婊”，对长辈不尊重，且“钟”“表”都有“终”的谐音
records.alternative: 送玉镯（平安健康）、佛珠（祈福吉祥）、平安扣（平安吉祥）

records.taboo: Watches should not be given to elders.
records.reason: "Watch" sounds like a derogatory term, which is disrespectful to elders. Both "clock" and "watch" have homophones related to "end."
records.alternative: Give jade bracelets (symbolizing peace and health), Buddhist prayer beads (symbolizing blessings and good fortune), or peace pendants (symbolizing peace and good luck).

records.taboo: 春节不送银色饰品
records.reason: 银色有“白银”之意，有“贫穷”的负面联想，春节讲究富贵吉祥
records.alternative: 送金色饰品（富贵吉祥）、红色饰品（喜庆吉祥）、黄色饰品（尊贵华丽）

records.taboo: Silver jewelry should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Silver is associated with "white silver," which has negative connotations of poverty, while the Spring Festival emphasizes wealth and good fortune.
records.alternative: Give gold jewelry (symbolizing wealth and good fortune), red jewelry (auspicious and festive), or yellow jewelry (noble and magnificent).

records.taboo: 不送十字架饰品给信佛教的人
records.reason: 十字架是基督教象征，与佛教信仰冲突，有不尊重对方信仰的嫌疑
records.alternative: 送佛珠（佛教吉祥）、玉佛（平安祈福）、莲花饰品（佛教象征）

records.taboo: Cross jewelry should not be given to Buddhists.
records.reason: The cross is a Christian symbol; giving it to a Buddhist conflicts with their faith and could be seen as disrespectful.
records.alternative: Give Buddhist prayer beads (auspicious for Buddhists), jade Buddha pendants (symbolizing peace and blessings), or lotus ornaments (a Buddhist symbol).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送风铃
records.reason: 风铃随风摆动，有“不稳定”的寓意，对新家安定不吉利
records.alternative: 送玉石摆件（平安吉祥）、水晶球（招财进宝）、吉祥挂件（祈福平安）

records.taboo: Wind chimes should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Wind chimes sway in the wind, implying "instability," which is inauspicious for the stability of a new home.
records.alternative: Give jade ornaments (symbolizing peace and good fortune), crystal balls (symbolizing attracting wealth), or auspicious hanging decorations (symbolizing blessings and peace).

records.taboo: 不送尖锐饰品给他人
records.reason: 尖锐饰品有“伤人”的寓意，易引起人际关系紧张，不吉利
records.alternative: 送圆润饰品（圆满和谐）、光滑饰品（顺利吉祥）、柔和饰品（温和友好）

records.taboo: Sharp-edged jewelry should not be given to others.
records.reason: Sharp edges imply "hurting others," which can cause tension in relationships and is inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give rounded jewelry (symbolizing harmony), smooth jewelry (symbolizing smoothness and good fortune), or soft-style jewelry (symbolizing gentleness and friendliness).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送黑色饰品
records.reason: 黑色象征哀悼，与婚礼的喜庆氛围不符，有“婚姻不幸”的暗示
records.alternative: 送红色饰品（喜庆吉祥）、金色饰品（富贵美满）、粉色饰品（浪漫温馨）

records.taboo: Black jewelry should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Black symbolizes mourning and does not fit the festive atmosphere of a wedding, implying marital misfortune.
records.alternative: Give red jewelry (auspicious and festive), gold jewelry (wealthy and happy), or pink jewelry (romantic and warm).

records.taboo: 不送破损饰品给他人
records.reason: 破损饰品有“残缺”之意，有“祝愿对方生活残缺”的不祥寓意
records.alternative: 送完好饰品（完整幸福）、精美饰品（美丽祝福）、吉祥饰品（祈福平安）

records.taboo: Damaged jewelry should not be given to others.
records.reason: Damaged items imply "imperfection," carrying the inauspicious meaning of "wishing an imperfect life upon the recipient."
records.alternative: Give intact jewelry (symbolizing complete happiness), exquisite jewelry (beautiful blessings), or auspicious jewelry (prayers for peace).

records.taboo: 春节不送骷髅头饰品
records.reason: 骷髅头象征死亡、不祥，与春节的喜庆氛围严重不符，极其不吉利
records.alternative: 送生肖饰品（本命年吉祥）、福字饰品（福气满满）、吉祥图案饰品（祈福平安）

records.taboo: Skull jewelry should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Skulls symbolize death and inauspiciousness, which is completely contrary to the festive atmosphere of the Spring Festival and is extremely unlucky.
records.alternative: Give zodiac animal ornaments (auspicious for one's birth year), "Fu" (blessing) character ornaments (full of blessings), or ornaments with auspicious patterns (prayers for peace).

records.taboo: 不送情侣饰品给单身朋友
records.reason: 情侣饰品有“提醒对方单身”的负面暗示，易让对方感到尴尬不适
records.alternative: 送个性饰品（展现自我）、吉祥饰品（祈福平安）、时尚饰品（美丽点缀）

records.taboo: Couple's jewelry should not be given to single friends.
records.reason: It implies a reminder that they are single, which can be embarrassing and uncomfortable for them.
records.alternative: Give personalized jewelry (showcasing individuality), auspicious jewelry (prayers for peace), or fashionable jewelry (beautiful accents).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送镜子饰品
records.reason: 镜子有“反射”之意，有“反射福气”的负面寓意，对新家不吉利
records.alternative: 送玉石摆件（平安吉祥）、陶瓷饰品（富贵美满）、木雕饰品（自然和谐）

records.taboo: Mirror ornaments should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Mirrors reflect, carrying the negative meaning of "reflecting away good fortune," which is inauspicious for a new home.
records.alternative: Give jade ornaments (symbolizing peace and good fortune), ceramic ornaments (wealthy and happy), or wood carving ornaments (natural harmony).

records.taboo: 不送宗教饰品给无宗教信仰的人
records.reason: 宗教饰品有特定信仰含义，送宗教饰品给无信仰者有强加信仰的嫌疑
records.alternative: 送时尚饰品（美丽点缀）、吉祥饰品（祈福平安）、个性饰品（展现自我）

records.taboo: Religious jewelry should not be given to non-religious people.
records.reason: It carries specific religious meanings; giving it to non-believers can be seen as imposing one's faith.
records.alternative: Give fashionable jewelry (beautiful accents), auspicious jewelry (prayers for peace), or personalized jewelry (showcasing individuality).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送珍珠饰品给再婚者
records.reason: 珍珠是“珠圆玉润”，但也有“旧珠”的联想，对再婚者不吉利
records.alternative: 送钻石饰品（永恒爱情）、黄金饰品（富贵吉祥）、玉石饰品（平安幸福）

records.taboo: Pearl jewelry should not be given to someone remarrying.
records.reason: While pearls symbolize perfection, they can also evoke the idea of "old pearls," which is inauspicious for someone entering a new marriage.
records.alternative: Give diamond jewelry (symbolizing eternal love), gold jewelry (symbolizing wealth and good fortune), or jade jewelry (symbolizing peace and happiness).

records.taboo: 不送廉价饰品给重要朋友
records.reason: 廉价饰品有“不重视对方”的负面暗示，易引起对方不满，不礼貌
records.alternative: 送品质饰品（真诚祝福）、品牌饰品（品质保证）、定制饰品（专属心意）

records.taboo: Cheap jewelry should not be given to important friends.
records.reason: Cheap jewelry implies that you don't value the other person, which can cause dissatisfaction and is impolite.
records.alternative: Give quality jewelry (sincere blessings), brand-name jewelry (quality assurance), or custom-made jewelry (personal sentiment).

records.taboo: 春节不送白色饰品
records.reason: 白色是丧葬用色，送白色饰品有“不祥”的寓意，春节讲究喜庆
records.alternative: 送红色饰品（喜庆吉祥）、金色饰品（富贵吉祥）、黄色饰品（尊贵华丽）

records.taboo: White jewelry should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: White is the color of mourning; giving white jewelry carries an inauspicious meaning, while the festival emphasizes festivity.
records.alternative: Give red jewelry (auspicious and festive), gold jewelry (wealthy and auspicious), or yellow jewelry (noble and magnificent).

records.taboo: 不送手表给恋人
records.reason: “表”谐音“婊”，有不尊重对方的嫌疑，且“钟”“表”有“终”的谐音
records.alternative: 送戒指（永恒承诺）、项链（心心相印）、手链（永结同心）

records.taboo: Watches should not be given to lovers.
records.reason: "Watch" sounds like a derogatory term, showing disrespect. Both "clock" and "watch" have homophones related to "end."
records.alternative: Give rings (symbolizing an eternal promise), necklaces (symbolizing mutual affection), or bracelets (symbolizing eternal unity).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送金属尖锐饰品
records.reason: 金属尖锐饰品有“伤人”的寓意，对家庭和谐不吉利
records.alternative: 送圆润饰品（圆满和谐）、木质饰品（自然温馨）、玉石饰品（平安吉祥）

records.taboo: Sharp metal ornaments should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Sharp metal items imply "hurting others," which is inauspicious for family harmony.
records.alternative: Give rounded ornaments (symbolizing harmony), wooden ornaments (natural and warm), or jade ornaments (symbolizing peace and good fortune).

records.taboo: 不送同名饰品给多人
records.reason: 同名饰品有“不专属”的寓意，易让对方感到不被重视，不礼貌
records.alternative: 送定制饰品（专属心意）、个性饰品（独特祝福）、不同款式饰品（专属选择）

records.taboo: Giving the same jewelry item to multiple people.
records.reason: Giving the same thing implies it's "not exclusive," making the recipient feel undervalued and is impolite.
records.alternative: Give custom-made jewelry (personal sentiment), personalized jewelry (unique blessings), or different styles (making each feel special).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送粗重饰品
records.reason: 粗重饰品有“沉重”之意，有“婚姻沉重”的负面联想，不吉利
records.alternative: 送轻巧饰品（轻松幸福）、精美饰品（美丽祝福）、吉祥饰品（祈福平安）

records.taboo: Heavy, bulky jewelry should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: It implies "heaviness," evoking the negative association of a "heavy marriage," which is inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give lightweight jewelry (symbolizing light and happy life), exquisite jewelry (beautiful blessings), or auspicious jewelry (prayers for peace).

records.taboo: 探病不送菊花
records.reason: 菊花是丧葬用花，送菊花给病人有“诅咒病人”的不祥寓意，极其不吉利
records.alternative: 送康乃馨（温馨关怀）、百合（纯洁美好）、郁金香（美好祝福）

records.taboo: Chrysanthemums should not be given when visiting the sick.
records.reason: Chrysanthemums are used for funerals; giving them to a patient carries the inauspicious implication of "cursing the patient" and is extremely unlucky.
records.alternative: Give carnations (symbolizing warm care), lilies (symbolizing purity and beauty), or tulips (symbolizing beautiful blessings).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送白色百合
records.reason: 白色百合虽象征纯洁，但也有“丧葬用白”的联想，对婚礼不吉利
records.alternative: 送红色玫瑰（热烈爱情）、粉色百合（浪漫温馨）、红色康乃馨（温馨祝福）

records.taboo: White lilies should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Although white lilies symbolize purity, they also evoke the color white associated with funerals, making them inauspicious for weddings.
records.alternative: Give red roses (symbolizing passionate love), pink lilies (romantic and warm), or red carnations (warm blessings).

records.taboo: 春节不送梅花
records.reason: 梅花在寒冬开放，有“苦寒”之意，春节讲究温暖吉祥，不宜送寒梅
records.alternative: 送牡丹（富贵吉祥）、兰花（高雅品质）、桃花（吉祥如意）

records.taboo: Plum blossoms should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Plum blossoms bloom in the harsh winter, symbolizing "hardship and cold," while the Spring Festival emphasizes warmth and good fortune.
records.alternative: Give peonies (symbolizing wealth and good fortune), orchids (symbolizing elegance), or peach blossoms (symbolizing good luck).

records.taboo: 不送含羞草给朋友
records.reason: 含羞草有“害羞、退缩”之意，有“祝愿对方退缩”的负面寓意
records.alternative: 送向日葵（积极向上）、太阳花（活力阳光）、波斯菊（快乐幸福）

records.taboo: Mimosa should not be given to friends.
records.reason: Mimosa has a meaning of "shyness and retreat," carrying the negative implication of "wishing the other to retreat."
records.alternative: Give sunflowers (symbolizing positivity), helianthus (vigorous sunshine), or cosmos (symbolizing happiness).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送仙人掌
records.reason: 仙人掌有尖刺，有“伤人”的寓意，对家庭和谐不吉利，易引起矛盾
records.alternative: 送绿萝（生机勃勃）、吊兰（清新空气）、富贵竹（富贵吉祥）

records.taboo: Cacti should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Cacti have sharp spines, implying "hurting others," which is inauspicious for family harmony and can cause conflict.
records.alternative: Give pothos (symbolizing vitality), spider plants (symbolizing fresh air), or lucky bamboo (symbolizing wealth and good fortune).

records.taboo: 不送昙花给他人
records.reason: 昙花花期短暂，有“美好短暂”的寓意，不利于长久关系，不吉利
records.alternative: 送月季（长久美丽）、菊花（长寿吉祥）、兰花（高雅长久）

records.taboo: Night-blooming cereus should not be given to others.
records.reason: Its bloom is fleeting, symbolizing "beauty that is short-lived," which is detrimental to lasting relationships and is inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give roses (symbolizing lasting beauty), chrysanthemums (symbolizing longevity and good fortune), or orchids (symbolizing lasting elegance).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送菊花
records.reason: 菊花是丧葬用花，与婚礼的喜庆氛围严重不符，极其不吉利
records.alternative: 送玫瑰（浪漫爱情）、百合（百年好合）、康乃馨（温馨祝福）

records.taboo: Chrysanthemums should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Chrysanthemums are funeral flowers; they clash severely with the festive atmosphere of a wedding and are extremely inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give roses (romantic love), lilies (symbolizing a harmonious union for a hundred years), or carnations (warm blessings).

records.taboo: 探病不送白色花
records.reason: 白色是丧葬用色，送白色花给病人有“不祥”的寓意，易引起病人不适
records.alternative: 送粉色花（温馨关怀）、黄色花（活力阳光）、红色花（喜庆吉祥）

records.taboo: White flowers should not be given when visiting the sick.
records.reason: White is the color of mourning; giving white flowers to a patient carries an "inauspicious" meaning and can cause discomfort.
records.alternative: Give pink flowers (warm care), yellow flowers (vigorous sunshine), or red flowers (auspicious and festive).

records.taboo: 不送彼岸花给他人
records.reason: 彼岸花是传说中的冥界之花，有“死亡、分离”的不祥寓意，极其不吉利
records.alternative: 送牡丹（富贵吉祥）、兰花（高雅品质）、桃花（吉祥如意）

records.taboo: Spider lilies (Lycoris radiata) should not be given to others.
records.reason: It is the legendary flower of the underworld, carrying the inauspicious meanings of "death and separation" and is extremely unlucky.
records.alternative: Give peonies (symbolizing wealth and good fortune), orchids (symbolizing elegance), or peach blossoms (symbolizing good luck).

records.taboo: 春节不送白色花
records.reason: 白色是丧葬用色，送白色花有“不祥”的寓意，春节讲究喜庆吉祥
records.alternative: 送红色花（喜庆吉祥）、黄色花（富贵尊贵）、粉色花（温馨浪漫）

records.taboo: White flowers should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: White is the color of mourning; giving white flowers carries an inauspicious meaning, while the festival emphasizes festivity and good fortune.
records.alternative: Give red flowers (auspicious and festive), yellow flowers (wealthy and noble), or pink flowers (warm and romantic).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送带刺植物
records.reason: 带刺植物有“伤人”的寓意，对家庭和谐不吉利，易引起矛盾冲突
records.alternative: 送无刺植物（和谐美满）、观叶植物（生机勃勃）、开花植物（吉祥如意）

records.taboo: Thorny plants should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Thorny plants imply "hurting others," which is inauspicious for family harmony and can cause conflict.
records.alternative: Give thornless plants (symbolizing harmony), foliage plants (symbolizing vitality), or flowering plants (symbolizing good luck).

records.taboo: 不送菊花给长辈
records.reason: 菊花是丧葬用花，送菊花给长辈有“诅咒长辈”的不祥寓意，极其不尊重
records.alternative: 送康乃馨（温馨关怀）、兰花（高雅品质）、牡丹（富贵吉祥）

records.taboo: Chrysanthemums should not be given to elders.
records.reason: Chrysanthemums are funeral flowers; giving them to an elder carries the inauspicious implication of "cursing the elder" and is extremely disrespectful.
records.alternative: Give carnations (warm care), orchids (elegance), or peonies (wealth and good fortune).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送白色玫瑰
records.reason: 白色玫瑰虽象征纯洁，但也有“丧葬用白”的联想，对婚礼不吉利
records.alternative: 送红色玫瑰（热烈爱情）、粉色玫瑰（浪漫温馨）、黄色玫瑰（友谊祝福）

records.taboo: White roses should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Although white roses symbolize purity, they also evoke the color white associated with funerals, making them inauspicious for weddings.
records.alternative: Give red roses (passionate love), pink roses (romantic and warm), or yellow roses (friendship blessings).

records.taboo: 不送仙人掌给恋人
records.reason: 仙人掌有尖刺，有“伤害感情”的寓意，不利于恋爱关系和谐
records.alternative: 送玫瑰（浪漫爱情）、百合（百年好合）、郁金香（美好祝福）

records.taboo: Cacti should not be given to lovers.
records.reason: Cacti have sharp spines, implying "hurting feelings," which is not conducive to a harmonious romantic relationship.
records.alternative: Give roses (romantic love), lilies (symbolizing a harmonious union for a hundred years), or tulips (beautiful blessings).

records.taboo: 春节不送菊花
records.reason: 菊花是丧葬用花，与春节的喜庆氛围严重不符，极其不吉利
records.alternative: 送牡丹（富贵吉祥）、兰花（高雅品质）、桃花（吉祥如意）

records.taboo: Chrysanthemums should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Chrysanthemums are funeral flowers; they clash severely with the festive atmosphere of the Spring Festival and are extremely inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give peonies (symbolizing wealth and good fortune), orchids (symbolizing elegance), or peach blossoms (symbolizing good luck).

records.taboo: 婚礼礼金不送300元
records.reason: 300元是单数，且“3”有“散”的谐音，有“婚姻分散”的不祥寓意
records.alternative: 送200元（成双成对）、600元（六六大顺）、800元（发发发）

records.taboo: 300 yuan should not be given as a wedding gift.
records.reason: 300 is an odd number, and "3" sounds like "scatter," implying the marriage might fall apart, which is inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give 200 yuan (symbolizing pairing), 600 yuan (symbolizing smoothness), or 800 yuan (symbolizing prosperity).

records.taboo: 春节红包不送40元
records.reason: “4”谐音“死”，有不祥寓意，春节红包讲究吉祥数字，避开4
records.alternative: 送20元（成双成对）、50元（五福临门）、60元（六六大顺）

records.taboo: 40 yuan should not be given as a Spring Festival red envelope.
records.reason: "4" sounds like "death," an inauspicious meaning. Spring Festival red envelopes favor lucky numbers, so avoid 4.
records.alternative: Give 20 yuan (symbolizing pairing), 50 yuan (symbolizing the arrival of five blessings), or 60 yuan (symbolizing smoothness).

records.taboo: 乔迁礼金不送500元
records.reason: 500元是单数，且“5”有“无”的谐音，有“无福无禄”的不祥寓意
records.alternative: 送600元（六六大顺）、800元（发发发）、1000元（千里挑一）

records.taboo: 500 yuan should not be given as a housewarming gift.
records.reason: 500 is an odd number, and "5" sounds like "none," carrying the inauspicious meaning of "no blessings or fortune."
records.alternative: Give 600 yuan (symbolizing smoothness), 800 yuan (symbolizing prosperity), or 1000 yuan (symbolizing being one in a thousand).

records.taboo: 探病礼金不送单数
records.reason: 单数有“孤单、不完整”的寓意，探病讲究祝福病人早日康复、家庭完整
records.alternative: 送200元（成双成对）、400元（四季平安）、600元（六六大顺）

records.taboo: Odd numbers should not be given as hospital visit gifts.
records.reason: Odd numbers imply "loneliness" and "incompleteness," while hospital visits emphasize wishes for the patient's recovery and family wholeness.
records.alternative: Give 200 yuan (symbolizing pairing), 400 yuan (symbolizing peace in all four seasons), or 600 yuan (symbolizing smoothness).

records.taboo: 不送破损的钱币
records.reason: 破损钱币有“残缺”之意，有“祝愿对方生活残缺”的不祥寓意，不礼貌
records.alternative: 送崭新钱币（全新祝福）、银行兑换的新钞（整洁吉祥）、红包包装（喜庆吉祥）

records.taboo: Damaged banknotes should not be given.
records.reason: Damaged money implies "imperfection," carrying the inauspicious meaning of "wishing an imperfect life upon the recipient," and is impolite.
records.alternative: Give crisp, new banknotes (brand-new blessings), new notes from the bank (clean and auspicious), and wrap them in a red envelope (festive and auspicious).

records.taboo: 婚礼礼金不送700元
records.reason: “7”谐音“凄”，有“凄凉、不幸”的寓意，对婚姻不吉利
records.alternative: 送600元（六六大顺）、800元（发发发）、900元（长长久久）

records.taboo: 700 yuan should not be given as a wedding gift.
records.reason: "7" sounds like "miserable," implying desolation and misfortune, which is inauspicious for the marriage.
records.alternative: Give 600 yuan (symbolizing smoothness), 800 yuan (symbolizing prosperity), or 900 yuan (symbolizing longevity).

records.taboo: 春节红包不送140元
records.reason: “14”谐音“要死”，有极其不祥的寓意，春节红包需绝对避开
records.alternative: 送120元（月月平安）、160元（六六大顺）、180元（发发发）

records.taboo: 140 yuan should not be given as a Spring Festival red envelope.
records.reason: "14" sounds like "will die," an extremely inauspicious meaning that must be absolutely avoided.
records.alternative: Give 120 yuan (symbolizing peace month by month), 160 yuan (symbolizing smoothness), or 180 yuan (symbolizing prosperity).

records.taboo: 乔迁礼金不送1300元
records.reason: “13”在西方是不吉利数字，在中国也有“失散”的谐音，不吉利
records.alternative: 送1200元（月月平安）、1600元（六六大顺）、1800元（发发发）

records.taboo: 1300 yuan should not be given as a housewarming gift.
records.reason: "13" is an unlucky number in the West, and in China it has a homophone meaning "lost" or "scattered," making it inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give 1200 yuan (symbolizing peace month by month), 1600 yuan (symbolizing smoothness), or 1800 yuan (symbolizing prosperity).

records.taboo: 不送硬币作为礼金
records.reason: 硬币面值小，有“不重视对方”的负面暗示，且携带不便，不礼貌
records.alternative: 送纸币（大方得体）、红包包装（喜庆吉祥）、银行转账（便捷整洁）

records.taboo: Coins should not be given as gift money.
records.reason: Coins have low face value, implying that you don't value the recipient; they are also inconvenient to carry, making it impolite.
records.alternative: Give banknotes (dignified and appropriate), use red envelopes (festive and auspicious), or use bank transfer (convenient and clean).

records.taboo: 婚礼礼金不送900元给再婚者
records.reason: “9”有“长久”之意，但对再婚者有“纪念前任”的负面联想，不吉利
records.alternative: 送800元（发发发）、1000元（全新开始）、1200元（月月平安）

records.taboo: 900 yuan should not be given to someone remarrying.
records.reason: While "9" symbolizes longevity, it can also evoke the negative association of "remembering the previous spouse," making it inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give 800 yuan (symbolizing prosperity), 1000 yuan (symbolizing a brand-new start), or 1200 yuan (symbolizing peace month by month).

records.taboo: 春节红包不送240元
records.reason: “24”谐音“饿死”，有极其不祥的寓意，春节红包需绝对避开
records.alternative: 送200元（成双成对）、260元（六六大顺）、280元（发发发）

records.taboo: 240 yuan should not be given as a Spring Festival red envelope.
records.reason: "24" sounds like "starve to death," an extremely inauspicious meaning that must be absolutely avoided.
records.alternative: Give 200 yuan (symbolizing pairing), 260 yuan (symbolizing smoothness), or 280 yuan (symbolizing prosperity).

records.taboo: 乔迁礼金不送400元
records.reason: “4”谐音“死”，有极其不祥的寓意，对新家发展不吉利
records.alternative: 送600元（六六大顺）、800元（发发发）、1000元（千里挑一）

records.taboo: 400 yuan should not be given as a housewarming gift.
records.reason: "4" sounds like "death," an extremely inauspicious meaning that is unlucky for the development of a new home.
records.alternative: Give 600 yuan (symbolizing smoothness), 800 yuan (symbolizing prosperity), or 1000 yuan (symbolizing being one in a thousand).

records.taboo: 探病礼金不送400元
records.reason: “4”谐音“死”，有极其不祥的寓意，对病人康复不吉利
records.alternative: 送200元（成双成对）、300元（三三不尽）、600元（六六大顺）

records.taboo: 400 yuan should not be given as a hospital visit gift.
records.reason: "4" sounds like "death," an extremely inauspicious meaning that is unlucky for the patient's recovery.
records.alternative: Give 200 yuan (symbolizing pairing), 300 yuan (symbolizing continuity), or 600 yuan (symbolizing smoothness).

records.taboo: 不送皱巴巴的礼金
records.reason: 皱巴巴的礼金有“不新鲜、不重视”的负面暗示，易引起对方不满
records.alternative: 送崭新礼金（全新祝福）、平整礼金（整洁吉祥）、红包包装（喜庆吉祥）

records.taboo: Wrinkled banknotes should not be given.
records.reason: Wrinkled money implies it's "not fresh" and that you don't care, easily causing dissatisfaction.
records.alternative: Give crisp, new banknotes (brand-new blessings), flat and neat banknotes (clean and auspicious), and wrap them in a red envelope (festive and auspicious).

records.taboo: 婚礼礼金不送1040元
records.reason: “104”谐音“要你死”，有极其恶毒的寓意，绝对不能送
records.alternative: 送1000元（千里挑一）、1200元（月月平安）、1600元（六六大顺）

records.taboo: 1040 yuan should not be given as a wedding gift.
records.reason: "104" sounds like "will cause you to die," an extremely malicious meaning that must never be given.
records.alternative: Give 1000 yuan (symbolizing being one in a thousand), 1200 yuan (symbolizing peace month by month), or 1600 yuan (symbolizing smoothness).

records.taboo: 不送手机给长辈
records.reason: 智能手机操作复杂，长辈难以使用，有“让长辈难堪”的负面暗示
records.alternative: 送老年机（简单实用）、平板电脑（大屏清晰）、智能手环（健康监测）

records.taboo: Mobile phones should not be given to elders.
records.reason: Smartphones are complicated for elders to use, implying a negative suggestion of "making the elder feel embarrassed."
records.alternative: Give senior-friendly phones (simple and practical), tablets (clear large screen), or smart wristbands (health monitoring).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送相机
records.reason: 相机用于拍照，有“记录不好瞬间”的负面联想，且“相”谐音“丧”
records.alternative: 送投影仪（家庭影院）、音响（美好音乐）、平板电脑（娱乐休闲）

records.taboo: Cameras should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Cameras are used to take pictures, evoking the negative idea of "recording bad moments." Additionally, "camera" sounds like "mourning."
records.alternative: Give projectors (home theater), speakers (beautiful music), or tablets (entertainment and leisure).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送路由器
records.reason: 路由器有“辐射”的负面联想，且“路由”有“路径”之意，有“限制发展”的暗示
records.alternative: 送空气净化器（清新空气）、加湿器（舒适环境）、智能音箱（便捷生活）

records.taboo: Routers should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Routers have negative associations with "radiation," and "router" implies "path," with a suggestion of "restricting development."
records.alternative: Give air purifiers (fresh air), humidifiers (comfortable environment), or smart speakers (convenient life).

records.taboo: 不送二手电子产品给他人
records.reason: 二手电子产品有“旧、坏”的负面暗示，有“不重视对方”的嫌疑
records.alternative: 送全新电子产品（全新祝福）、品牌电子产品（品质保证）、实用电子产品（贴心关怀）

records.taboo: Used electronic products should not be given to others.
records.reason: Used electronics imply they are "old and possibly faulty," suggesting that you don't value the recipient.
records.alternative: Give brand-new electronics (brand-new blessings), brand-name electronics (quality assurance), or practical electronics (thoughtful care).

records.taboo: 春节不送闹钟
records.reason: 闹钟用于叫醒，有“打扰休息”的寓意，春节讲究放松休息，且“钟”谐音“终”
records.alternative: 送智能手环（健康监测）、电子日历（记录时光）、平板电脑（娱乐休闲）

records.taboo: Alarm clocks should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Alarm clocks are used to wake people, implying "disturbing rest," while the festival emphasizes relaxation. Also, "clock" sounds like "end."
records.alternative: Give smart wristbands (health monitoring), electronic calendars (marking time), or tablets (entertainment and leisure).

records.taboo: 不送游戏机给孩子
records.reason: 游戏机易让孩子沉迷，有“耽误学习”的负面暗示，家长通常不喜欢
records.alternative: 送学习机（辅助学习）、点读笔（学习工具）、平板电脑（学习娱乐两用）

records.taboo: Game consoles should not be given to children.
records.reason: Game consoles can easily cause addiction, implying "hindering studies," and parents usually dislike them.
records.alternative: Give educational computers (learning aid), reading pens (learning tool), or tablets (for both learning and entertainment).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送打印机
records.reason: 打印机用于打印文件，有“打印离婚协议”的负面联想，极其不吉利
records.alternative: 送投影仪（家庭影院）、音响（美好音乐）、相机（记录幸福）

records.taboo: Printers should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Printers are used for printing documents, evoking the negative association of "printing divorce papers," which is extremely inauspicious.
records.alternative: Give projectors (home theater), speakers (beautiful music), or cameras (capturing happiness).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送微波炉
records.reason: 微波炉用于加热，有“加热矛盾”的负面联想，对家庭和谐不吉利
records.alternative: 送电饭煲（丰衣足食）、电烤箱（美味生活）、空气炸锅（健康饮食）

records.taboo: Microwaves should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Microwaves are used for heating, evoking the negative idea of "heating up conflicts," which is inauspicious for family harmony.
records.alternative: Give rice cookers (abundant food), ovens (delicious life), or air fryers (healthy eating).

records.taboo: 不送电脑给学生
records.reason: 电脑易让学生沉迷游戏、网络，有“耽误学习”的负面暗示
records.alternative: 送学习机（辅助学习）、平板电脑（学习娱乐两用）、电子词典（学习工具）

records.taboo: Computers should not be given to students.
records.reason: Computers can easily lead students to indulge in games and the internet, implying "hindering studies."
records.alternative: Give educational computers (learning aid), tablets (for both learning and entertainment), or electronic dictionaries (learning tool).

records.taboo: 春节不送扫地机器人
records.reason: 扫地机器人用于清洁，有“扫走福气”的寓意，春节讲究保留福气
records.alternative: 送空气净化器（清新空气）、加湿器（舒适环境）、智能音箱（便捷生活）

records.taboo: Robot vacuums should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Robot vacuums are used for cleaning, implying "sweeping away good fortune," while the festival emphasizes retaining good fortune.
records.alternative: Give air purifiers (fresh air), humidifiers (comfortable environment), or smart speakers (convenient life).

records.taboo: 不送录音笔给他人
records.reason: 录音笔用于录音，有“监视、偷听”的负面联想，易引起严重误会
records.alternative: 送钢笔（文化气质）、笔记本（实用记录）、U盘（便捷存储）

records.taboo: Voice recorders should not be given to others.
records.reason: Voice recorders are used for recording, evoking negative ideas of "surveillance" or "eavesdropping," which can cause serious misunderstandings.
records.alternative: Give fountain pens (symbolizing cultural taste), notebooks (practical for recording), or USB drives (convenient storage).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送手机
records.reason: 手机用于通讯，有“联系外人”的负面联想，不利于婚姻忠诚
records.alternative: 送情侣手表（虽然表有禁忌，但可送象征时间永恒的）、平板电脑（共同娱乐）、投影仪（家庭影院）

records.taboo: Mobile phones should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Mobile phones are for communication, evoking the negative idea of "contacting outsiders," which is not conducive to marital fidelity.
records.alternative: Give couple's watches (though watches have taboos, consider ones symbolizing eternal time), tablets (for shared entertainment), or projectors (home theater).

records.taboo: 乔迁不送电视
records.reason: 电视有“屏幕”之意，有“屏障”的负面联想，对家庭交流不吉利
records.alternative: 送投影仪（家庭影院）、音响（美好音乐）、空气净化器（清新空气）

records.taboo: Televisions should not be given when moving to a new home.
records.reason: Television has a "screen," evoking the negative idea of a "barrier," which is inauspicious for family communication.
records.alternative: Give projectors (home theater), speakers (beautiful music), or air purifiers (fresh air).

records.taboo: 不送电子烟给他人
records.reason: 电子烟仍有健康风险，送电子烟有“危害对方健康”的负面暗示，不负责
records.alternative: 送茶叶（健康饮品）、茶具（品味生活）、保健品（健康关怀）

records.taboo: E-cigarettes should not be given to others.
records.reason: E-cigarettes still pose health risks; giving them implies "harming the recipient's health" and is irresponsible.
records.alternative: Give tea (healthy beverage), tea sets (tasteful living), or health supplements (health care).

records.taboo: 春节不送电脑
records.reason: 电脑用于工作，有“春节还要工作”的负面暗示，春节讲究休息放松
records.alternative: 送平板电脑（娱乐休闲）、智能手环（健康监测）、电子阅读器（阅读休闲）

records.taboo: Computers should not be given during the Spring Festival.
records.reason: Computers are for work, implying "having to work during the Spring Festival," while the festival emphasizes rest and relaxation.
records.alternative: Give tablets (entertainment and leisure), smart wristbands (health monitoring), or e-readers (reading and leisure).

records.taboo: 不送智能门锁给乔迁者
records.reason: 智能门锁有“安全隐患”的负面联想，对新家安全不吉利，易引起担忧
records.alternative: 送普通门锁（安全可靠）、门铃（温馨提示）、监控摄像头（安全保障）

records.taboo: Smart door locks should not be given to those moving to a new home.
records.reason: Smart door locks have negative associations with "security risks," which is inauspicious for the safety of a new home and can cause worry.
records.alternative: Give standard door locks (safe and reliable), doorbells (courteous notification), or security cameras (safety assurance).

records.taboo: 婚礼不送音响
records.reason: 音响声音大，有“吵闹”的负面联想，对婚姻和谐不吉利
records.alternative: 送耳机（私人音乐）、投影仪（家庭影院）、平板电脑（共同娱乐）

records.taboo: Speakers should not be given at weddings.
records.reason: Speakers produce loud sound, evoking the negative idea of "noise and quarreling," which is inauspicious for marital harmony.
records.alternative: Give headphones (private music), projectors (home theater), or tablets (shared entertainment).

records.taboo: 不送充电宝给恋人
records.reason: 充电宝用于充电，有“补充能量”之意，但也有“随时需要充电”的负面联想
records.alternative: 送情侣手链（永结同心）、项链（心心相印）、戒指（永恒承诺）

records.taboo: Power banks should not be given to lovers.
records.reason: Power banks are for charging, implying "replenishing energy," but also carry the negative idea of "needing to be recharged all the time."
records.alternative: Give couple's bracelets (symbolizing eternal unity), necklaces (symbolizing mutual affection), or rings (symbolizing an eternal promise).
